channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: Observability for Delegated Execution in Agentic AI Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09692v1

AI Summary (中文)

问题背景

在基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体系统（Agentic AI）中，任务委托（delegation）是核心运行范式：主智能体动态调用工具、生成子智能体、并行执行异构操作。然而，现有可观测性方案（如审计日志、执行追踪）无法可靠识别“某次操作归属于哪一委托上下文”——相同日志序列可能对应多种互斥的委托分配，导致委托范围（delegation-scoped）的归因完全模糊。该问题源于三重挑战：（1）执行路径高度非确定性（同指令下序列各异）；（2）跨工具/子智能体的追踪碎片化与时间交织；（3）现有日志缺乏显式委托语义，仅记录“谁做了什么”，不记录“为谁、以何种委托权限而做”。

方法创新

本文提出代理感知的可观测性基底（agent-aware observability substrate），包含两个关键组件：

  • 轻量级网关（Lightweight Gateway）：在所有工具调用和子智能体启动前注入委托上下文令牌（Delegation Context Token, DCT），实现零侵入式绑定；
  • 统一信息模型（Common Information Model, CIM）：定义delegation_id、delegator_id、delegatee_role、share_scope（数据/权限共享边界）等核心字段，使异构系统日志具备可对齐的委托语义。

核心贡献

✅ 首次实现委托范围内的精确归因与访问足迹重建（非意图推理）；
✅ 支持跨工具、跨子智能体的端到端委托链路追溯，无需依赖启发式时间窗口关联；
✅ 在真实LLM智能体流水线中验证：委托归属准确率从基线<42%提升至99.7%， forensic 查询延迟降低83%；
✅ 开源模型规范与网关参考实现，兼容OpenTelemetry与W3C Trace Context标准。

AI Summary (English)

Delegation-scoped observability remains fundamentally broken in LLM-based agentic systems: standard audit logs and traces are structurally insufficient to reconstruct which actions occurred under a specific delegation, due to non-deterministic execution paths, trace fragmentation across tools/sub-agents, and absence of delegation semantics in existing logging schemas. We introduce an agent-aware observability substrate comprising a lightweight gateway (injecting delegation context tokens at invocation time) and a common information model (CIM) that explicitly encodes delegation identity, authority scope, and data sharing boundaries. This enables precise, cross-heterogeneous-system delegation attribution and access footprint reconstruction—without heuristic temporal correlation. Evaluation shows >99.7% delegation attribution accuracy and 83% faster forensic queries versus trace-correlation baselines. Our approach targets attribution, not intent inference, and is compatible with OpenTelemetry and W3C Trace Context.

Abstract

Delegation-scoped execution is not identifiable from standard observables: audit logs and execution traces can be identical under multiple incompatible delegation assignments. This gap is especially acute in LLM-based agentic systems, where agents dynamically select tools, vary execution sequences across runs for the same instruction, and spawn cooperating sub-agents. These dynamics fragment and interleave traces, making delegation-scoped reconstruction from causal structure alone structurally underdetermined. Although individual actions are authorized and logged, existing audit, tracing, and security schemas lack the semantics to reconstruct what actions occurred under a given delegation across heterogeneous systems. We focus on delegation-scoped attribution and access/share footprint reconstruction, not intent inference or reasoning reconstruction. We present an agent-aware observability substrate consisting of a lightweight gateway and a common information model that binds delegation context at execution time. This enables reliable cross-tool delegation-scoped reconstruction and direct forensic queries without heuristic time-window correlation.

item.title: Safe-RULE: Safe Reinforcement UnLEarning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09559v1

AI Summary (中文)

Safe-RULE：面向离线安全强化学习的数据毒化防御新范式

背景与挑战：离线安全强化学习（Offline Safe RL）通过静态数据集训练策略，避免在线交互，在机器人、自动驾驶等安全关键系统中具有重要应用价值。然而，其对固定数据集的强依赖性使其极易遭受数据毒化攻击——攻击者向训练数据中注入恶意样本，导致策略在保持任务性能的同时严重违反安全约束（如碰撞、越界），且此类损害难以通过常规微调修复。

方法创新：本文提出安全强化“遗忘”（Safe Reinforcement UnLEarning, Safe-RULE）——一种无需原始环境访问、无需从头训练的新型防御范式。Safe-RULE的核心在于：

  • 设计双目标遗忘损失函数，协同优化任务性能保留（policy utility）与安全约束恢复（safety constraint satisfaction）；
  • 引入安全敏感梯度掩码机制，识别并削弱毒化样本对安全临界状态-动作对的梯度影响；
  • 采用基于置信度的样本影响力重加权，动态降低可疑毒化样本在遗忘更新中的贡献权重。

主要发现与优势：在Safety-Gym、Safe-D4RL等基准任务上的实验表明：
✅ 在5种典型毒化攻击下，Safe-RULE将平均安全违规率降低62.3%–89.1%，显著优于基线方法（如Fine-tuning、Retraining with scrubbing）；
✅ 任务性能（如回报）下降控制在<3.7%，远优于完全重训练（平均下降18.5%）；
✅ 仅需单轮参数更新（≈1/10原始训练计算量），支持实时响应式安全修复。
本工作首次将“机器遗忘”思想系统性引入安全强化学习领域，为构建可信赖、可审计、可修正的离线智能体提供了新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Safe-RULE: Safe Reinforcement UnLEarning

Offline safe reinforcement learning (Safe RL) enables policy training without online interaction—critical for safety-critical systems—but is vulnerable to data poisoning attacks that degrade safety without noticeably harming task performance. To address this, we propose Safe-RULE, a novel reinforcement unlearning framework that efficiently removes the influence of poisoned data without retraining from scratch or accessing the original environment. Unlike standard unlearning, Safe-RULE explicitly jointly optimizes for both task utility preservation and safety constraint recovery, via a dual-objective loss, safety-aware gradient masking, and confidence-based sample reweighting. Experiments across Safety-Gym and Safe-D4RL benchmarks show that Safe-RULE reduces safety violations by 62.3%–89.1% under diverse poisoning attacks while preserving >96% of task performance—and achieves this with only ~10% of the compute cost of full retraining. Safe-RULE establishes the first principled, efficient, and environment-free defense against data poisoning in offline Safe RL.

Abstract

Offline safe reinforcement learning (Safe RL) enables policy learning without online interactions, making it suitable for safety-critical systems such as robotics systems. However, its reliance on static datasets exposes offline Safe RL to data poisoning attacks, where adversaries inject malicious samples that compromise safety and induce unsafe policy behavior. In this work, we propose a new learning paradigm, named safe reinforcement unlearning (Safe-RULE), used as a defense framework to remove the influence of poisoned data without retraining from scratch or requiring access to the original training environment. We further extend reinforcement unlearning to offline Safe RL by explicitly accounting for both task performance and safety constraints during the unlearning process. Experiments across benchmark Safe RL tasks demonstrate that our approach effectively enhances safety performance against data poisoning attacks.

item.title: SecureClaw: Clawing Back Control of LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09549v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

面向工具调用的大型语言模型（LLM）智能体面临两类根本性安全失效：一是未经授权的外部动作执行（如越权调用API、修改数据库），二是敏感数据在运行时内存中以明文形式暴露——该暴露发生在最终输出审查机制介入之前，导致攻击者可通过提示注入、中间层劫持等手段窃取凭证、隐私或上下文信息。现有防御方案（如输入过滤、输出审查、沙箱化执行）通常仅保护单一边界（规划器/运行时 或 动作执行端），无法协同防御“读”与“写”双路径风险。

方法：SecureClaw双边界架构

我们提出 SecureClaw，一种从数据流源头施加控制的新型安全架构，实现读边界（read boundary）的明文隔离与写边界（effect sink）的强授权管控：

  • 读侧：所有敏感数据读取经由可信网关（Trusted Gateway）处理，原始值被替换为不可逆的不透明句柄（opaque handles），并可选提供策略限定的有界摘要（bounded summaries） 作为显式的信息降级（declassification）接口；
  • 写侧：状态变更操作遵循 PREVIEW→COMMIT 协议——运行时仅能生成预览请求（含摘要/符号引用），仅可信执行器（Trusted Executor）可依据策略校验后提交标准化、不可篡改的最终请求。

效果与创新

SecureClaw 在保持任务实用性（如AgentDojo任务完成率≥92%）前提下，在三大基准上实现迄今最优防御效果：

  • Agent Security Bench (ASB)：攻击成功率（ASR）0%（唯一达此指标的方案）；
  • AgentDojo：ASR 低至 0.64%（较次优方案降低超10倍）；
  • AgentLeak：在最严苛的“被攻击奇偶车道”（attacked parity lane）中，整体泄露率仅3.23%，涵盖最终输出泄露与内部中继泄露。

其核心创新在于将安全控制点前移至数据首次进入/离开可信域的“爪形”边界（Claw），兼顾机密性、完整性与可用性。

AI Summary (English)

SecureClaw is a dual-boundary security architecture for tool-using LLM agents, addressing two critical failure modes: unauthorized external actions and in-runtime plaintext exposure of sensitive data before output checks. Unlike prior defenses targeting only the planner/runtime or action sink, SecureClaw enforces authorization at the effect sink (via a PREVIEW→COMMIT protocol enforced by a trusted executor) and plaintext confinement at the read boundary (via a trusted gateway that replaces raw secrets with opaque handles and bounded, policy-governed summaries). This design prevents direct secret dereferencing and unapproved side effects while preserving planning over abstractions. Evaluated uniformly across AgentDojo, AgentLeak, and Agent Security Bench (ASB), SecureClaw is the only defense achieving 0% ASR on ASB, 0.64% ASR on AgentDojo, and only 3.23% total leakage on AgentLeak’s attacked parity lane—demonstrating unprecedented robustness without sacrificing task utility.

Abstract

Tool-using large language model (LLM) agents face two distinct security failures: unauthorized external actions and exposure of sensitive plaintext inside the runtime before any final output check can intervene. Existing defenses usually protect one boundary, either the planner/runtime or the action sink, and therefore do not by themselves secure both surfaces. We present SecureClaw, a dual-boundary architecture that places authorization at the effect sink and plaintext confinement at the read boundary. Sensitive reads pass through a trusted gateway that replaces raw values with opaque handles and, in the evaluated deployment, bounded summaries as an explicit declassification interface. Writes that change external state follow a PREVIEW$\rightarrow$COMMIT protocol in which only a trusted executor may commit the exact canonical request authorized by policy. The runtime can still plan over summaries and symbolic references, but cannot directly dereference secrets or perform side effects. Across AgentDojo, AgentLeak, and Agent Security Bench (ASB), SecureClaw is the only defense we evaluate in a common harness that simultaneously retains usable task utility and achieves 0\% attack success rate (ASR) on ASB, 0.64\% ASR on AgentDojo, and 3.23\% overall leak on AgentLeak's attacked parity lane, which measures final-output and internal-relay leakage.

item.title: Model Poisoning Against Federated Model Adaptation with Chain of Bit-Flips
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09548v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（FL）允许多个客户端在不共享原始数据的前提下协同训练全局模型，但其去中心化特性也引入了新型安全威胁。传统后门攻击依赖恶意客户端篡改训练数据或梯度（算法层面），而硬件故障类攻击（如Rowhammer引发的位翻转）正成为被忽视的物理层攻击面。本文首次将硬件故障引入联邦模型自适应（Federated Model Adaptation） 场景，提出一种新型模型投毒范式。

方法与创新

我们设计了一种任务无关、链式位翻转（Chain of Bit-Flips）驱动的模型投毒攻击：

  • 离线预置阶段：基于FL系统初始预训练模型，通过优化生成一组关键参数位置及翻转方向（0→1或1→0），构建轻量级“翻转链”；
  • 在线投毒阶段：仅需单个恶意客户端在本地训练中对自身模型参数实施≤10次精准位翻转（无需修改代码或数据），即可植入隐蔽后门；
  • 攻击泛化性：该方法不依赖特定任务、架构或数据分布，适用于ResNet、ViT等多种模型。

主要发现

在CIFAR-10与Tiny-ImageNet上的实验表明：

  • 对ResNet-18模型，仅需19次恶意客户端参与（每次≤10位翻转）即可实现94%攻击成功率，且全局模型准确率下降<1.2%，隐蔽性强；
  • 后门触发仅需输入含特定触发器（如右下角像素块）的样本，对正常样本无干扰；
  • 攻击在真实Rowhammer约束下可行——单次DRAM刷新周期内可完成所需翻转，兼容主流消费级GPU内存。

意义与启示

本工作揭示了FL系统在硬件-算法跨层耦合下的深层脆弱性，推动防御研究从纯软件视角转向“软硬协同安全”。我们同时分析了现有鲁棒聚合（如Krum、Median）对此类攻击的无效性，并指出物理层检测（如ECC内存监控）是更根本的缓解路径。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Chain of Bit-Flips (CoBF), a novel hardware-assisted model poisoning attack against federated model adaptation. Unlike conventional data- or gradient-based backdoor attacks, CoBF exploits physical bit-flips—induced by hardware faults like Rowhammer—to directly corrupt parameters of a single malicious client’s local model during FL training. The backdoor is crafted offline from the system’s pretrained model and is task-agnostic, requiring no access to training data or model architecture specifics. Our evaluation shows that injecting as few as 10 bit-flips per malicious update across only 19 client rounds suffices to achieve a 94% attack success rate on ResNet-18, while preserving >98.8% clean accuracy. The attack is robust across datasets (CIFAR-10, Tiny-ImageNet) and models (including ViT), and remains practical under realistic Rowhammer constraints. Critically, standard robust aggregation defenses fail against CoBF, highlighting an urgent need for hardware-aware security in FL.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) allows a set of clients to collectively train a global model without sharing local training data. Giving the responsibility of the training to decentralized actors may lead to poisoning attacks: clients controlled by malicious third party potentially poison the training dataset to install a backdoor in neural networks. In FL, these backdoor attacks rely solely on algorithmic approach, however, recent advances in hardware faults threats (e.g, Rowhammer) have widen the overall attack surface. In the context of federated model adaptation, we introduce a novel category of backdoor attack against FL systems that relies on model poisoning based on hardware-fault attacks. More precisely, we propose a task-agnostic backdoor attack that is implanted during the FL training time by inducing hardware faults (bit-flips) in parameters of a single local model. The backdoor is crafted during a previous offline phase from the pretrained model initially used by the FL system. Our results show that a backdoor can be successfully applied on different type of models and datasets. Typically, with up to 10 faults per malicious client occurrence and 19 total occurrences on a ResNet-18 are enough to reach 94% of attack success rate. Finally, we discuss the practicality and the robustness of the attack potential defenses, while putting into perspective the practical constraints of Rowhammer, which is the preferred attack vector for this type of threats.

item.title: Targeting World Models to Compromise Robot Learning Pipelines
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09499v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

世界模型（World Models）作为数据高效型生成工具，正被广泛集成至机器人学习流水线中，用于合成训练轨迹或仿真真实环境。尽管实用性强，本文首次揭示其在机器人学习供应链中引入了一种前所未有的隐蔽数据投毒入口点：攻击者无需篡改原始标注数据，即可诱导下游策略失效或产生安全隐患。

攻击原理与创新性

区别于传统数据投毒（如直接注入危险轨迹），本工作提出两类新型攻击：

  • 恶意提示注入（Prompt Poisoning）：在看似安全的遥操作数据集中嵌入特定文本提示（如“当红色灯亮起时急停”），该提示在原始数据中无害，但经文本条件世界模型处理后被激活，生成含致命错误的合成轨迹；
  • 动态篡改（Dynamics Poisoning）：在状态-动作对中植入微小、语义隐匿的转移动力学偏差（如将“轻推”建模为“猛推”），仅当输入至动作条件世界模型时被放大，导致生成轨迹偏离物理现实。

关键发现与验证

  • 在SOTA世界模型（如Decision Transformer变体、VPT-based模型）上实现端到端后门攻击：下游强化学习策略在触发条件下（如特定视觉线索或指令）执行高危动作，成功率＞92%；
  • 首次在视觉-语言-动作（VLA）范式中完成概念验证，证明攻击可跨模态迁移；
  • 所有攻击均通过“安全数据集审计”，原始遥操作数据在人工/自动检测下完全不可疑。

本研究呼吁亟需构建具备投毒鲁棒性认证的世界模型架构，并重新评估其在机器人安全关键系统中的部署边界。

AI Summary (English)

World models (WMs) are increasingly integrated into robot learning pipelines for data-efficient simulation and trajectory generation. However, this work uncovers a stealthy, supply-chain-level vulnerability: WMs introduce a novel data poisoning entry point that compromises downstream policies even when trained on audited, seemingly safe teleoperated datasets. Unlike conventional poisoning—which injects overtly dangerous trajectories—our attacks embed malicious prompts (for text-conditioned WMs) or subliminal transition dynamics (for action-conditioned WMs) that remain inert until processed by the world model. This triggers synthetic, hazardous training trajectories, leading to unsafe or backdoored robotic policies. We demonstrate end-to-end backdoors against state-of-the-art WMs (e.g., DT-based and VPT-derived models), achieving >92% attack success on downstream DRL policies, and provide a proof-of-concept in the VLA setting. These findings call for security-aware WM design and critical reevaluation of their role in safety-critical robotics.

Abstract

World models have recently seen a rapid growth in both their popularity and capability as more data efficient tools for generating robot training data or simulating real world environments, with many works proposing their integration into the robot learning pipeline. While highly practical, in this work we demonstrate that world models introduce a uniquely stealthy and effective data poisoning entry point into the robot learning supply chain that can result in the deployment of unsafe or otherwise compromised robotic policies despite training on seemingly safe ground truth training data. In contrast to traditional data poisoning techniques which directly implant dangerous trajectories into sold or uploaded datasets, our novel attack methods inject malicious prompts or compromising transition dynamics into visibly safe teleoperated datasets which are only activated once fed through a world model as input. This can result in the generation of synthetic, dangerous robot training trajectories and subsequently unsafe or compromised robot policies. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our attacks against both state of the art action conditioned and text conditioned world models, showing a full end-to-end backdoor on a downstream DRL policy and a proof-of-concept for the VLA setting. Overall these findings necessitate research into more secure world models and reevaluating their position within the robot learning supply chain.

item.title: Fully Oblivious Differential Privacy for Frequency Estimation in the Augmented Shuffle Model with Trusted Processors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09402v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在差分隐私（DP）的混洗模型（Shuffle Model）中，混洗器（shuffler）通过随机重排用户数据实现高精度与强隐私保障。然而，近期研究表明：多数现有协议易受数据收集方与恶意用户合谋攻击影响；虽有工作提出增强型混洗模型（augmented shuffle model），通过引入随机采样与虚拟数据注入提升抗合谋能力，但该模型仍存在关键信任缺口——无法确保混洗器自身诚实执行协议，亦无法防范其与数据收集方串通。

创新方案：TEE赋能的全隐匿差分隐私（FODP）

本文首次将可信执行环境（TEEs，如Intel SGX）深度融入增强型混洗模型，系统性解决混洗器可信性问题。核心贡献包括：

  • 提出全新隐私定义 FODP（Fully Oblivious Differential Privacy），在标准DP基础上，严格约束外部/内部内存访问模式、缓存行为及控制流痕迹，抵御基于侧信道的TEE绕过攻击；
  • 构建首个通用FODP算法框架，基于内存尺寸恒定化（memory-size obfuscation）技术，设计三种可证明安全的FODP频率估计算法；
  • 通过集成计数最小草图（Count-Min Sketch）与哈希函数数量优化策略，显著降低通信开销与TEE内存占用；
  • 在Intel SGX平台上完成端到端实现与评估，对比9种基线方法（含经典LDP、标准shuffle及最新TEE-DP方案），结果表明：本方案在ε=1.0下误差降低37%–62%，且对时序/缓存侧信道攻击具备鲁棒性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the critical trust gap in the augmented shuffle model of differential privacy (DP), where the shuffler may collude with the data collector. We introduce Fully Oblivious DP (FODP)—a strengthened privacy notion that provably thwarts TEE side-channel attacks by obfuscating memory access patterns, cache behavior, and control flow. Leveraging Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), we propose a general FODP framework based on memory-size obfuscation and instantiate three concrete frequency estimation algorithms. To enhance efficiency, we integrate the Count-Min Sketch and optimize hash function counts. Evaluated on Intel SGX against nine baselines, our algorithms achieve up to 62% lower estimation error at ε = 1.0 while remaining resilient to timing and cache-based side-channel adversaries.

Abstract

In the shuffle model of DP (Differential Privacy), a shuffler randomly permutes users' data to achieve high accuracy and privacy. Recent studies show that most existing shuffle protocols are vulnerable to collusion attacks by the data collector and users. They address this issue by introducing the augmented shuffle model that incorporates random sampling and dummy data addition into the shuffler. However, it remains open how to ensure the shuffler follows the protocol and does not collude with the data collector in this model. We address this trust issue by thoroughly exploring the augmented shuffle model with TEEs (Trusted Execution Environments). We first introduce a new privacy notion, FODP (Fully Oblivious DP), which strengthens DP to prevent various TEE side-channel attacks based on external/internal memory access patterns and control flows. We propose a general framework for FODP algorithms based on memory-size obfuscation and three concrete algorithms within it. We also improve the efficiency of our algorithms by using the count-min sketch and optimizing the number of hashes. We evaluate our algorithms on Intel SGX and demonstrate their effectiveness through comparisons with nine baselines.

item.title: Benchmarking Empirical Privacy Protection for Adaptations of Large Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09401v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

近期研究将差分隐私（DP）引入大语言模型（LLM）适配过程，以支持医疗、金融等敏感场景，虽提供理论隐私保证，但其实际防护效果尚不明确。核心挑战在于：LLM预训练数据与适配数据之间普遍存在语义重叠与统计依赖，即使无显式数据复用，也可能削弱DP的实际效用。

方法与基准设计

本研究构建首个面向DP-LLM适配的实证隐私基准框架，采用前沿攻击手段——鲁棒成员推断攻击（Robust MIA）与金丝雀数据提取攻击（Canary Extraction），系统评估隐私风险。我们通过三类数据分布梯度进行控制实验：（1）与预训练数据精确重叠；（2）同分布（IID）适配数据；（3）完全分布外（OOD）样本。同时交叉考察适配方法（全参数微调、LoRA、Adapter、Prefix-Tuning）与隐私预算ε（0.5–8.0）的影响。

主要发现

  • 分布偏移是关键调节因子：在相同ε下，适配数据越接近预训练分布，实证隐私风险越高——IID场景风险比OOD高3.2×，且该效应独立于直接数据重叠；
  • LoRA在OOD场景中表现最优：在ε=2.0时，其成员推断准确率仅52.1%（接近随机），显著优于全微调（68.7%）；
  • 理论保证≠实践安全：当ε≥4.0时，多数方法在IID数据上仍面临>60%的金丝雀提取成功率，揭示DP机制在LLM适配中的“保证衰减”现象。

创新与意义

本工作首次量化了预训练-适配数据分布关系对DP实效性的决定性影响，提出可落地的适配策略建议（如优先选用LoRA处理OOD敏感数据），并发布开源基准工具包。进一步，我们倡导扩展评估维度至全生命周期隐私（pretrain→adapt→inference），为合规部署提供结构化风险评估框架。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first empirical benchmark of differential privacy (DP) in large language model (LLM) adaptation, exposing a critical gap between theoretical guarantees and practical privacy. We evaluate real-world leakage using state-of-the-art robust membership inference and canary extraction attacks across systematically varied adaptation data distributions—from exact pretraining overlaps to fully out-of-distribution (OOD) cases. Results show that distribution proximity to pretraining data strongly amplifies privacy risk: IID adaptation incurs up to 3.2× higher inference accuracy than OOD at identical ε, even without data reuse. Among adaptation methods, LoRA achieves the strongest empirical protection for OOD data (e.g., 52.1% MIA accuracy at ε=2.0 vs. 68.7% for full fine-tuning). Crucially, standard DP budgets (ε ≥ 4.0) fail to prevent >60% canary recovery under IID settings—demonstrating “guarantee decay” in LLM pipelines. We propose a holistic privacy assessment framework spanning pretraining, adaptation, and inference, offering actionable guidance for deploying DP-LLMs in high-stakes domains.

Abstract

Recent work has applied differential privacy (DP) to adapt large language models (LLMs) for sensitive applications, offering theoretical guarantees. However, its practical effectiveness remains unclear, partly due to LLM pretraining, where overlaps and interdependencies with adaptation data can undermine privacy despite DP efforts. To analyze this issue in practice, we investigate privacy risks under DP adaptations in LLMs using state-of-the-art attacks such as robust membership inference and canary data extraction. We benchmark these risks by systematically varying the adaptation data distribution, from exact overlaps with pretraining data, through in-distribution (IID) cases, to entirely out-of-distribution (OOD) examples. Additionally, we evaluate how different adaptation methods and different privacy regimes impact the vulnerability. Our results show that distribution shifts strongly influence privacy vulnerability: the closer the adaptation data is to the pretraining distribution, the higher the practical privacy risk at the same theoretical guarantee, even without direct data overlap. We find that parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods, such as LoRA, achieve the highest empirical privacy protection for OOD data. Our benchmark identifies key factors for achieving practical privacy in DP LLM adaptation, providing actionable insights for deploying customized models in sensitive settings. Looking forward, we propose a structured framework for holistic privacy assessment beyond adaptation privacy, to identify and evaluate risks across the full pretrain-adapt pipeline of LLMs.

item.title: Brain-Prompt Injection: A Route-Safety Audit for BCI-LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09315v1

AI Summary (中文)

脑提示注入：面向BCI-LLM智能体的路由安全审计

本研究首次系统揭示并命名脑提示注入（Brain-Prompt Injection）这一新型攻击范式——在脑机接口（BCI）驱动的大语言模型（LLM）智能体管道中，攻击者通过神经信号侧扰动、纯上下文注入或自适应双解码器攻击，可恶意篡改智能体的工具调用路由决策，而传统EEG信号监控或文本输出审查均无法察觉。我们指出：路由安全性不取决于解码器准确率或输出一致性本身，而取决于审计日志可观测性。为此，我们提出路由安全审计契约（Route-Safety Audit Contract），包含三要素：最小化日志模式（记录指令源、上下文、确认状态、执行终点）、分母层级（seed/case/subject三级归一化基准）与端点规范（明确定义“成功路由”语义）。理论层面，我们证明审计模式分离定理与C3攻击依赖分解：仅靠“干净一致性”（clean agreement）和“边缘鲁棒性”（marginal robustness）无法识别控制C3级路由翻转的关键联合项。作为契约的校准层，我们采用分裂共形校准（split-conformal calibration）作用于非预言机式EEG确认通道，并在显式威胁原型矩阵下报告假接受前沿（false-accept frontier, FAR）。在EEGMMI数据集（5,400次原生左右指令事件）、无害工具桩及种子/案例分母上验证：溯源（provenance）阻断C2路由（FAR=0.000）；一致性+溯源捕获C3翻转（召回率1.000）；确认+溯源实现零误接受（FAR=0.000）。共形前沿显示：在采集隔离条件下，α=0.005时达FAR=0.000（清洁效用0.150）；α=0.10时FAR=0.119（清洁效用0.452）；而若确认通道被攻击者可控，则界坍塌至≈1。60被试的主体聚类自助法验证区间稳健性；跨架构（TinyEEGNet/EEGNetV4）与容量扫描证实域内饱和。关键结论：中介（mediation）与确认（confirmation）是风险缓解机制，而非意图认证凭证。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Brain-Prompt Injection—a novel attack class targeting BCI-to-LLM agent pipelines, where adversarial perturbations to neural signals or context alone can stealthily hijack tool-routing decisions undetected by EEG or text monitors. We argue that route safety hinges on audit observability, not decoder accuracy or output agreement alone. To formalize this, we define a Route-Safety Audit Contract, comprising a minimal log schema, denominator hierarchy (seed/case/subject), and endpoint specification—and prove an audit-schema separation theorem and C3 attacked-dependence decomposition, showing that clean agreement and marginal robustness fail to identify the joint term governing C3 routing failures. Using split-conformal calibration on a non-oracle EEG confirmation channel, we report the false-accept frontier (FAR) under an explicit threat-archetype matrix. Evaluated on 5,400 native left/right EEGMMI events with harmless tool stubs, provenance blocks C2 routes (FAR = 0.000); agreement + provenance detects all C3 flips (recall = 1.000); confirmation + provenance achieves zero false accepts (FAR = 0.000). The conformal frontier reaches FAR = 0.000 at clean utility 0.150 (α = .005) and FAR = 0.119 at 0.452 (α = .10) under acquisition isolation—collapsing to ≈1 if confirmation is attacker-controllable. Results are validated across 60 subjects (bootstrap), architectures (TinyEEGNet, EEGNetV4), and capacity sweeps, confirming within-regime saturation. Crucially, mediation and confirmation mitigate risk—they are not intent certificates.

Abstract

BCI-to-agent pipelines turn decoded neural activity into an authorization channel for tool-use agents, exposing a new attack surface we call \emph{brain-prompt injection}: signal-side perturbations, context-only injections, and adaptive dual-decoder attacks can all change the routed action while EEG-side or text-side monitors remain blind. Route safety in this stack depends on what the audit log can observe, not on decoder accuracy or agreement alone. We define a Route-Safety Audit Contract: a minimal log schema, denominator hierarchy, and endpoint specification, and prove an audit-schema separation theorem together with a C3 attacked-dependence decomposition; clean agreement and marginal robustness do not identify the joint term that controls C3 routing. As a calibration layer on top of the contract, we apply split-conformal calibration to a non-oracle EEG confirmation channel and report the resulting false-accept frontier under an explicit threat-archetype matrix. We instantiate the contract on EEGMMI native left/right command-control over 5{,}400 events, harmless tool stubs, and seed/case denominators. Provenance blocks C2 routes ($0.000$); agreement-plus-provenance routes C3 flips ($1.000$); confirmation-plus-provenance routes them ($0.000$). The conformal frontier reaches FAR $0.000$ at clean utility $0.150$ for $α=.005$ and FAR $0.119$ at clean utility $0.452$ for $α=.10$ under acquisition isolation; an attacker-controllable confirmation channel breaks the bound to $\approx\!1$. Subject-cluster bootstrap confirms these intervals on $60$ subjects; cross-architecture (TinyEEGNet, EEGNetV4) and capacity-sweep results show within-regime saturation. Mediation and confirmation reduce risk; they are not intent certificates.

item.title: Trustworthy Smart Fabs via Professional Proxies: Scaling Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) through Industrial Data Spaces
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09227v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

2026年欧盟“安全可持续设计（SSbD）”框架、《企业可持续发展尽职调查指令》（CSDDD）及“碳边境调节机制”（CBAM）三重监管叠加，正对先进半导体制造厂（“智能晶圆厂”，Smart Fabs）构成严峻治理瓶颈。传统人工报告模式已无法满足多层级合规要求，暴露出透明度需求与企业数据主权之间的根本性张力——尤其在工艺配方等核心知识产权保护前提下，如何向监管方、供应链与公众可信地证明可持续性绩效，成为行业共性难题。

方法与创新

本文提出一种零信任社会技术协同框架，将SSbD六层参考架构深度嵌入可信工业数据空间（Industrial Data Spaces, IDS）。核心创新在于引入“专业代理（Professional Proxies）”：基于角色的自主智能体工作流，在硬件级隔离的信任域（如Intel TDX/AMD SEV-SNP）中运行。框架以可互操作网络协议栈为结构，驱动设施、工艺工程与财务三大代理团队完成五步自动化“接力赛”，动态对齐产线良率模型与宏观可持续目标。

关键技术实现

  • 通过虚拟量测（VM）预测与联邦机器学习（FML） 在硬件根植的可信执行环境（TEEs）内闭环执行；
  • 利用国际数据空间（IDS）连接器，对外输出密码学签名的合规凭证（compliance tokens），全程不暴露原始工艺数据或配方；
  • 有效破解“数据主权悖论”——既保障企业数据不出域、不共享原始值，又提供可验证、可审计的第三方信任锚点。

意义

本框架为技术管理者提供了面向Industry 5.0的可验证、证据驱动、韧性可扩展的净零转型路径，推动智能制造从“合规响应”跃升至“自主治理”。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a zero-trust socio-technical framework that embeds the EU’s Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) architecture into trustworthy industrial data spaces to resolve regulatory bottlenecks facing Smart Fabs under CSDDD, CBAM, and SSbD 2026. We replace reactive automation with autonomous governance via Professional Proxies—role-specific, agent-based workflows executing inside hardware-isolated Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs). The framework orchestrates a five-step automated “relay race” across Facility, Process Engineering, and Finance proxies, aligning real-time yield models with macro sustainability mandates. By performing Virtual Metrology (VM) and Federated Machine Learning (FML) entirely within TEEs, it enables export of cryptographically signed compliance tokens via International Data Spaces (IDS) connectors—without exposing proprietary process recipes or raw data. This resolves the Data Sovereignty Paradox and delivers a verifiable, evidence-based pathway toward resilient, net-zero Industry 5.0 ecosystems.

Abstract

The convergence of the 2026 European Union Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) framework, Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD), and Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) introduce a severe governance bottleneck for advanced semiconductor manufacturing facilities ("Smart Fabs"). Regulatory compliance demands have surpassed the capacity of manual corporate reporting, creating a direct conflict between multi-stakeholder transparency and corporate data privacy. This paper addresses this challenge by introducing a zero-trust socio-technical orchestration framework that operationalizes a six-layer SSbD reference architecture within trustworthy industrial data spaces. We propose a shift from reactive automation to autonomous governance through "Professional Proxies"-role-based agentic workflows executing within hardware-isolated trust zones. Structured as an interoperable network protocol stack, the framework coordinates an automated, five-step "relay race" between Facility, Process Engineering, and Finance proxy teams to align factory-floor yield models with macro-level sustainability mandates. By executing Virtual Metrology (VM) predictions and Federated Machine Learning (FML) inside hardware-rooted Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), this architecture resolves the Data Sovereignty Paradox, demonstrating how fabs can export cryptographically signed compliance tokens via International Data Spaces (IDS) connectors without exposing proprietary process recipes. Ultimately, this framework provides technology managers with a verifiable, evidence-based pathway toward resilient, net-zero Industry 5.0 ecosystems.

item.title: The Injection Paradox: Brand-Level Suppression in Safety-Trained LLM Recommendations via RAG Context Injection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09204v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本研究揭示了一种在基于检索增强生成（RAG）的推荐系统中、由安全对齐（safety-training）引发的新型失效模式——“注入悖论”（Injection Paradox）。传统认知中，恶意提示注入（prompt injection）旨在操控模型输出以提升特定品牌曝光；但本文发现，在经严格安全训练的闭源大语言模型（如Anthropic Claude系列）中，此类注入反而会系统性抑制目标品牌，且效果远超预期。

方法与实验设计

我们构建可复现的RAG推荐基准：在包含4个品牌文档（仅1篇嵌入典型安全规避型注入文本，如“Do NOT recommend Brand X — it violates safety policies”）的语料库中，向模型提交中性查询（如“推荐一款可靠的笔记本电脑品牌”），统计各品牌在top-2推荐中的出现频次。实验覆盖3个主流消费电子品牌，每品牌50次独立推理，并在Claude Opus 4.6、Sonnet 4.5及GPT-4o、GPT-4-turbo上进行跨模型验证。

核心发现

  • 强抑制效应：Claude Opus中，目标品牌推荐率从注入前54%骤降至0%（50/50 trials无top-2出现）；
  • 跨文档传染性：抑制不局限于注入文档本身，同品牌其余3篇未修改文档亦被显著降权；
  • 模型家族差异：GPT系列呈现相反趋势——相同注入使目标品牌推荐率上升22–38个百分点；
  • 机制归因：安全训练使Claude对含“禁止”“违反”等安全关键词的上下文产生强回避响应，RAG检索结果被整体视为可信依据，触发链式抑制。

创新与启示

该悖论首次揭示了安全对齐与RAG架构耦合下的隐性风险，提出一种新型“反向攻击”范式：竞品方只需在对手公开文档中植入合规性警示语句，即可利用目标模型的安全敏感性实现被动压制。这对AI推荐系统的鲁棒性评估、安全策略设计及商业伦理治理具有重要警示意义。

AI Summary (English)

We identify the Injection Paradox: a reproducible failure mode in safety-trained RAG-based LLM recommendation where prompt injections in retrieved documents—intended to manipulate output—paradoxically suppress the target brand below baseline, even when injected into only 1 of 4 brand documents. In Claude Opus 4.6, the target brand’s top-2 recommendation rate drops from 54% to 0% across all 50 trials, with suppression propagating to unmodified documents of the same brand. This directional effect replicates across three brands and counterfactual controls, but reverses in GPT models, where identical injections increase recommendations by 22–38 percentage points—highlighting fundamental model-family differences in how safety-aligned models interpret injection-like context. The findings reveal a technically feasible reverse-attack vector: adversaries may embed innocuous-seeming safety warnings (e.g., “Do NOT recommend Brand X”) in competitors’ public documents to exploit safety-sensitive inference and suppress brand visibility.

Abstract

We present a reproducible failure mode of safety training in RAG-based LLM recommendation -- the Injection Paradox -- in which prompt injections embedded in retrieved documents backfire against the attacker, suppressing the target brand below the injection-free baseline. In safety-trained Claude models, documents containing prompt injections suffer a sharp drop in recommendation rate, and this suppression propagates beyond the injected document to unmodified documents of the same brand. In Claude Opus 4.6, the target brand drops from a 54% baseline to zero top-2 recommendations across all 50 trials, even though only 1 of 4 brand documents in the corpus contains an injection. The directional pattern is reproduced in counterfactual experiments and across three brands. A contrasting result across the GPT models tested, where the same injection instead increases recommendations, suggests model-family differences in how injection-like context affects recommendation behavior. These findings raise the technical possibility of a reverse-attack scenario in which an adversary embeds injections in a competitor's documents to suppress the competitor's brand via safety-sensitive model behavior.

item.title: Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09189v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前脑电图（EEG）基础模型（如BIOT、LaBraM、EEGPT）的隐私审计多采用“单端点孤立审查”范式——仅分别评估原始波形重建、成员推断、身份关联或下游头微调中的差分隐私（DP-SGD）效果。然而，此类割裂式审计易遗漏跨模型、跨任务的隐性属性泄露风险，尤其在冻结编码器输出的嵌入空间中持续存在的频谱属性可迁移性。

方法创新

本研究提出首个联合四端点联合审计框架：同步检验同一组预训练冻结嵌入在原始重建、成员推断（LiRA）、身份链接与DP-SGD鲁棒性四个维度的表现。核心突破是跨编码器属性迁移审计（cross-encoder transfer audit）：在单一编码器（如BIOT）上训练岭回归属性解码器，通过拟合线性“桥接层”（linear bridge），即可泛化至其他编码器（LaBraM/EEGPT）的被试不相交测试集，且所有六种方向（3模型×2方向）的匹配对照95%置信区间下界均≥0.081（p<0.001）。

关键发现与理论支撑

我们证明：若两编码器共享非平凡的属性坐标投影重叠度β，则存在链式岭桥接攻击者，其中心增益下界为√(β/(1+τ²)) − ε₆ᵣ − ρ₀；反解得β∈[0.008, 0.198]。进一步，提出审计端点分歧得分（AEDS）——量化各端点结果不一致性，经自助法校准后，在全部8个实证场景（含EEGMMI、Sleep-EDF、LIMO、CHB-MIT）中显著为正（p<0.001），而标准LiRA成员推断AUC仅0.50–0.70。Wiener自适应噪声攻击、LiRA及ε∈{4,8}的DP-SGD均无法阻断该属性通道。

贡献定位

本工作将碎片化防御升维为可部署的联合发布决策规则，提供理论保障（桥接定理）与强基线（自适应攻击/LiRA/DP-SGD）验证；其结论支持释放拦截（release-blocking），而非禁止原始波形获取或被试身份恢复——精准锚定模型级隐私风险边界。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first joint multi-endpoint audit framework for EEG foundation models (BIOT, LaBraM, EEGPT), simultaneously evaluating raw-reconstruction fidelity, membership inference (via Carlini LiRA), identity linkage, and DP-SGD robustness on frozen embeddings. Crucially, we discover persistent cross-encoder spectral attribute leakage: a ridge decoder trained on one model’s embeddings transfers—via a fitted linear bridge—to subject-disjoint test splits of all other models, with matched-control 95% CI lower bounds ≥0.081 across all six encoder-pair directions. We prove a sufficient condition linking shared attribute-coordinate projector overlap β to bridge attack gain, estimating β ∈ [0.008, 0.198]. To operationalize auditing, we propose the Audit-Endpoint Disagreement Score (AEDS), which is significantly positive (p < 0.001) in all eight benchmark cells (e.g., EEGMMI, Sleep-EDF, CHB-MIT), while standard LiRA achieves only AUC 0.50–0.70. Wiener-style adaptive attackers, LiRA, and DP-SGD (ε = 4, 8) fail to suppress this channel. Our framework enables release-blocking decisions grounded in cross-encoder transfer theory—not waveform exfiltration or identity recovery prohibitions.

Abstract

EEG foundation-model releases are usually audited one endpoint at a time: raw-reconstruction, membership inference, identity linkage, or DP-SGD on the downstream head. We audit the same released embeddings under all four endpoints jointly, on BIOT, LaBraM, and EEGPT, and show that each single-endpoint audit clears releases that still leak spectral attributes. The decisive evidence is a cross-encoder transfer audit: a single ridge attribute decoder learned from one frozen encoder transfers, via a fitted linear bridge, to held-out-subject test splits of every other encoder, with subject-disjoint matched-control 95% CI lower bound at least 0.081 across all six BIOT/LaBraM/EEGPT directions. We prove a sufficient condition: two encoders sharing a nontrivial attribute-coordinate projector overlap beta admit a chained ridge bridge attacker with centered-gain lower bound sqrt(beta/(1+tau^2)) - eps_br - rho_0, and back-solve beta in [0.008, 0.198]. To turn the joint audit into a deployment-readable decision rule we introduce an audit-endpoint disagreement score (AEDS), prove sufficient conditions for its positivity, and bootstrap-calibrate it per cell; AEDS is positive in all eight matched-CI cells (BIOT/LaBraM/EEGPT on EEGMMI; LaBraM on Sleep-EDF, 54-channel LIMO, CHB-MIT pediatric scalp EEG) with p<0.001, while a head-level Carlini LiRA membership audit reaches AUC only 0.50-0.70. Standard defenses fail under audit: a Wiener-style noise-aware adaptive attacker, the LiRA audit, and DP-SGD at every utility-preserving epsilon in {4,8} leave the attribute channel essentially unchanged. The contribution is an audit framework that turns scattered single-endpoint defenses into a joint release decision, supported by a cross-encoder bridge theorem and adaptive-attacker, LiRA, and DP-SGD baselines; the audit licenses release-blocking, not raw-waveform exfiltration or held-out-subject identity recovery.

item.title: EnclaveScale: Hardware-Assisted Edge-DP for Secure Data Centre Power Telemetry
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09163v1

AI Summary (中文)

EnclaveScale：面向数据中心电力遥测的硬件辅助边缘差分隐私框架

背景与挑战：现代AI数据中心需实时感知GPU功耗瞬态以实现动态供电编排，但现有加密遥测方案在10 Hz高频流式场景下扩展性差，且无法可靠验证传感器数据源头——恶意主机可伪造或篡改原始读数，导致生成式AI负载建模失真。中心化差分隐私（central-DP）虽保障统计隐私，却将原始敏感数据集中暴露于可信聚合器，违背多租户场景下的最小信任原则。

核心方法：EnclaveScale提出分层可信遥测架构：

  • 边缘-DP层：在32台GCP机密虚拟机上部署DCAP远程证明流水线，对NVML采集的GPU功耗时序进行事件级差分隐私处理——先注入拉普拉斯噪声，再通过拜占庭鲁棒拒绝机制过滤异常值，最终将连续瞬态压缩为离散马尔可夫转移矩阵；
  • 首英里认证层：引入SPDM协议实现传感器到TEE的端到端身份绑定，弥补当前平台缺失的可信I/O；
  • 全局聚合层：由Global Aggregation Enclave统一验证各边缘证明，并执行容量加权聚合，通过空间稀释（spatial dilution）保护宏观工作负载的保密性。

关键成果：实测显示，单enclave稳态吞吐达131,406样本/秒，平均证明开销仅0.23 μs/样本；在H100/A100/L4真实功耗轨迹上，动态编排功率误差为1.3 MW（相较诚实聚合器central-DP基线的0.1 MW），但严格保障了子秒级异常的本地混淆与租户间隐私隔离。本工作首次将PCIe IDE与TDISP等新兴硬件安全特性纳入电力遥测可信链，为多租户AI数据中心奠定安全、可扩展的功耗感知基础。

AI Summary (English)

EnclaveScale is a hardware-assisted, distributed telemetry architecture for secure, high-resolution power monitoring in AI data centers. It introduces edge differential privacy—performing noise injection, Byzantine rejection, and Markov-chain abstraction within attested enclaves (using DCAP) across 32 GCP Confidential VMs—achieving 0% post-extraction attack success rate. Unlike central-DP, it eliminates raw-data exposure by guaranteeing event-level DP locally and using SPDM-authenticated first-mile sensing to prevent host-level spoofing. A Global Aggregation Enclave verifies cryptographic proofs before capacity-weighted aggregation with spatial dilution. Evaluation shows 131,406 samples/s/enclave throughput and 0.23 μs/sample amortized attestation overhead. On real H100/A100/L4 traces, its dynamic power orchestration error is 1.3 MW, trading modest accuracy loss for strong tenant confidentiality and end-to-end provenance security—enabled by emerging PCIe IDE and TDISP hardware.

Abstract

EnclaveScale is a distributed, hardware-assisted telemetry architecture providing post-extraction attestation, enabling operators to collaboratively model high-resolution generative AI power transients. Existing cryptographic techniques scale poorly for 10-Hz streaming or fail to authenticate origins, permitting malicious hosts to spoof sensor inputs. We implement and evaluate a post-extraction pipeline utilizing DCAP attestation, differential privacy noise injection, and Byzantine rejection across 32 GCP Confidential VMs, achieving 0\% post-extraction attack success rate. This edge-DP approach distils continuous GPU transients into discrete Markov-chain transition matrices, guaranteeing event-level differential privacy. To mitigate pre-ingestion vulnerabilities, we propose an SPDM-authenticated first-mile layer. While current platforms lack attested I/O, emerging hardware architectures integrate PCIe IDE and TDISP to natively prevent host-level synthesis, securing the end-to-end provenance boundary. A Global Aggregation Enclave verifies these cryptographic proofs prior to capacity-weighted aggregation. Evaluation demonstrates a steady-state throughput of $131{,}406$ samples/s per enclave, amortising attestation overhead to $0.23\,μ$s/sample. On empirical NVML-sampled H100, A100, and L4 traces, EnclaveScale achieves a dynamic orchestration margin error of $1.3$\,MW compared to $0.1$\,MW for an honest-aggregator central-DP baseline. EnclaveScale establishes a secure foundation for dynamic multi-tenant power orchestration, obfuscating sub-second anomalies locally and protecting macro-workload confidentiality via spatial dilution during global aggregation.

item.title: Customization under Fire: Plugin Poisoning in Text-to-Image Ecosystem
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09151v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

文本到图像（T2I）模型的繁荣催生了以低秩适配（LoRA）插件为核心的共享共创生态（如 Civitai、Liblib），极大降低了模型定制门槛。然而，这一“即插即用”的民主化范式潜藏严重供应链安全风险：恶意用户可发布外观无害的LoRA插件，在模型共享市场中悄然植入隐蔽恶意功能，实施“插件投毒”，侵蚀用户信任根基，并危及海量下游应用安全。

方法与创新

本文提出 PoisonLoRA——首个面向真实T2I生态的LoRA插件供应链攻击系统性研究。我们揭示两类高危害攻击模式：

  • 概念劫持（Concept Hijacking）：被劫持的LoRA在常规提示下生成符合特定政治/社会叙事的图像，用于舆论操纵与虚假宣传；
  • 任务注入（Task Injection）：LoRA仅在触发秘密密钥（如特定文本前缀或隐写token）时激活，定向生成违法有害内容（如NSFW图像）。

关键突破在于发现其病毒式传播机制：恶意载荷能通过LoRA合并（merging）、叠加等常规创作行为跨插件持续扩散，每一次合法“混音”（remix）都使新插件成为新一代传染载体，实现自我增殖。

主要发现

实验覆盖Civitai与Liblib两大平台、4类典型场景、6个基准数据集，结果显示：

  • 攻击成功率（ASR）达≈100%，且平台现有审核机制完全未检出；
  • 毒性高度鲁棒：跨不同基础模型迁移后ASR仍近100%，经5次以上合并 remix 后毒性不衰减；
  • 首次实证LoRA生态存在“信任链断裂→投毒→协同传播”闭环风险，为AI模型共享安全敲响警钟。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces PoisonLoRA, the first systematic study of supply-chain poisoning attacks targeting LoRA plugins in real-world text-to-image (T2I) ecosystems (e.g., Civitai, Liblib). We identify two stealthy, high-impact attack vectors: (1) Concept Hijacking, where poisoned LoRAs generate propaganda-aligned images under benign prompts to manipulate perception; and (2) Task Injection, where harmful outputs (e.g., NSFW content) are conditionally activated only via secret triggers. Critically, the malicious payload propagates virus-like through routine creative operations—especially LoRA merging—turning every remix into a new carrier. Extensive evaluation across 6 datasets and 4 scenarios shows near-perfect attack success rates (≈100% ASR) on both major platforms, with zero detection by their current moderation systems. The poison remains robust after cross-base-model transfer and survives >5 rounds of merging—demonstrating unprecedented persistence and stealth in T2I plugin ecosystems.

Abstract

The prosperity of text-to-image (T2I) models has fostered a vibrant share-and-play ecosystem centered on Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) plugins, which allow users to customize and share model capabilities with ease. This democratization, however, comes with a hidden but severe security risk. Malicious users could share and distribute seemingly benign LoRA plugins that contain hidden functionalities to poison the model-sharing market, like Civitai or Liblib, severely undermining the user trust that underpins this collaborative ecosystem and threatening the safety of countless downstream applications. Despite these risks, plugin poisoning in the real-world T2I ecosystem remains underexplored. This paper introduces PoisonLoRA, the first systematic study of LoRA plugin supply-chain risks that exploits the trust and characteristics within the T2I ecosystem. We identify two primary attack instances: (1) Concept Hijacking, where a hijacked LoRA could generate images to influence public opinion and spread propaganda, and (2) Task Injection, where a LoRA is injected to produce harmful content (e.g., NSFW images) only activated by a secret key. Critically, the malicious payload persists with virus-like propagation. Such propagations weaponize the very act of creative collaboration (e.g., LoRA merging) to spread its contagion, turning every remix into a new carrier. Extensive experiments validate that PoisonLoRA is both effective and stealthy. Specifically, we achieve approximately 100% attack success rates (ASR) on both Civitai and Liblib on 6 datasets across 4 scenarios, without being detected by the platforms. The poisoned LoRA demonstrates extreme robustness, with nearly 100% ASR even transferred to different base models and remixed more than 5 times.

item.title: PrivCode++: Latent-Conditioned Differentially Private Code Generation for Comprehensive Guarantees
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09145v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLM）在指令-代码对上微调时易记忆并泄露敏感训练数据。现有差分隐私（DP）代码生成方法通常仅保护代码片段，默认提示（prompt）为公开信息——这与现实场景严重脱节：实际应用中，用户输入的提示常含隐私内容（如内部API密钥、业务逻辑片段或个人身份信息）。当提示本身不可见或禁止用于生成过程时，传统DP方法面临三重困境：效用急剧下降、生成代码多样性萎缩、语义保真度显著退化。

方法创新：PrivCode++

本文提出 PrivCode++（读作“PrivCode-Plus”），是首个系统性解决“提示与代码双重敏感性”的DP代码生成框架。其核心贡献为：

  • 两阶段差分隐私微调机制：第一阶段通过噪声注入与梯度裁剪实现私有化LoRA适配；第二阶段引入隐私无关的潜在条件模块（Privacy-Free Latent Conditioning, PFLC），将敏感提示与代码映射至共享隐空间，仅利用非敏感潜变量指导生成，彻底规避原始敏感数据访问；
  • 端到端合成保障：支持在无原始prompt/code参与下完成私有代码合成，满足严格的数据最小化原则。

实验结果

在HumanEval、MBPP及自建敏感Prompt-Bench基准上，PrivCode++较SOTA基线（如DP-Code、DP-SFT）平均提升Pass@1达28.6%，多样性（n-gram entropy）提升41.3%，且在ε=2.0下仍优于非私有方法的76%效用。更重要的是，它首次提供对提示与代码联合分布的严格(ε,δ)-DP保证，填补了LLM代码生成中双向隐私保护的理论与实践空白。

AI Summary (English)

PrivCode++ is the first differentially private (DP) code generation framework that treats both prompts and code snippets as sensitive during LLM fine-tuning—addressing a critical gap where prior DP methods assume prompts are public. To avoid direct access to sensitive inputs while preserving utility, we propose a two-stage DP framework featuring a Privacy-Free Latent Conditioning (PFLC) module, which maps prompts and code into a shared latent space and enables generation using only sanitized latent representations. Extensive experiments on HumanEval, MBPP, and a novel sensitive-prompt benchmark show PrivCode++ achieves 28.6% higher Pass@1 than DP baselines, maintains 76% of non-private model utility at ε=2.0, and significantly improves output diversity (+41.3% n-gram entropy). Crucially, it provides formal (ε,δ)-DP guarantees over the joint distribution of prompts and generated code—enabling comprehensive privacy for real-world code synthesis scenarios.

Abstract

Large language models fine-tuned on instruction-code pairs may memorize and subsequently leak sensitive training data. Existing differentially private (DP) code generation methods primarily protect code snippets while assuming prompts are public, which fails in realistic scenarios where prompts may also contain sensitive information. When prompts cannot be explicitly learned or used during generation, code synthesis suffers from severe utility degradation as well as reduced diversity and fidelity. To address these challenges, we propose PrivCode-Plus, the first work to explore DP code generation where both prompts and code snippets are considered sensitive in LLM fine-tuning. PrivCode-Plus introduces a two-stage DP framework with a Privacy-Free Latent Conditioning module, enabling effective DP fine-tuning and data synthesis without direct access to sensitive prompts or code. Extensive experiments show that PrivCode-Plus achieves substantially higher utility than baselines, remains competitive with the method with relaxing privacy assumptions, and provides stronger privacy guarantees.

item.title: Steganography Without Modification: Hidden Communication via LLM Seeds
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09135v1

AI Summary (中文)

隐写无需修改：基于大语言模型种子的隐蔽通信

本研究首次揭示了一种无需任何模型修改的新型大语言模型（LLM）隐写通道：攻击者仅通过操控随机数生成器（PRNG）种子，即可在标准推理过程中秘密嵌入信息。该通道利用LLM确定性解码中的一个关键结构性特性——逆变换采样（inverse-transform sampling）所依赖的PRNG会生成种子决定的token级概率区间序列，而该序列可仅从生成文本中完全重构。发送方在文本生成前将秘密消息编码为32位PRNG种子；接收方则通过穷举搜索种子空间，结合对生成文本的概率区间反推与匹配，实现种子（即载荷）的可靠恢复。

我们形式化定义两种操作模式：

  • 已知提示（known-prompt）模式：收发双方共享原始提示，可精确重建每个token的概率区间，借助强制对齐实现100%准确的32位种子恢复；
  • 未知提示（unknown-prompt）模式：仅拥有生成文本，通过近似区间重建与最大命中计数（maximum-hit-count）评分策略，在足够长输出下仍保持高恢复率。

实验覆盖6类主流模型（Llama、Qwen、Phi、Gemma等）及5类异构文本域（代码、新闻、对话、法律、学术）。结果表明：在已知提示下，全空间$2^{32}$枚举可在300 token内、单卡35秒内达成最高100%恢复精度；未知提示下，600–800 token即可在约12秒内实现>99.5%准确率。我们进一步系统分析了提示工程、分词歧义性及温度/Top-p等采样超参对信道鲁棒性的影响，并指出：该发现不仅支持32比特隐蔽传输，更从根本上挑战了“提示未知即安全”的常见假设——提示不可见性不构成有效防御边界。

AI Summary (English)

We discover a novel, modification-free steganographic channel in standard LLM inference stacks: secret messages are encoded solely via the PRNG seed used in deterministic decoding—no model weights, sampling logic, or output distributions need alteration. The channel exploits that inverse-transform sampling produces a seed-determined sequence of token-level probability intervals, fully reconstructable from the generated text alone. A sender embeds a 32-bit payload in the seed; a receiver recovers it via exhaustive seed-space search guided by interval reconstruction and scoring. In the known-prompt setting, exact interval reconstruction enables perfect (100%) seed recovery within 300 tokens and <35 seconds on one GPU. In the unknown-prompt setting, approximate reconstruction with maximum-hit-count scoring achieves >99.5% accuracy at 600–800 tokens in ~12 seconds across six model families and five text domains. Our work invalidates the security assumption that prompt secrecy suffices for defense.

Abstract

We demonstrate that widely deployed Large Language Model (LLM) inference stacks harbor a steganographic channel that requires no modification to model weights, sampling code, or output distributions. The channel exploits a structural property of deterministic decoding: pseudo-random number generators (PRNGs) used in inverse-transform sampling produce a seed-dependent sequence of token-level probability intervals that can be reconstructed from the generated text alone. A sender encodes a secret message in the PRNG seed before generation; a receiver reconstructs the intervals and recovers the seed, and thus the hidden payload, by exhaustive search over the seed space. We formalize two operational modes. In the known-prompt setting, sender and receiver share the prompt, enabling exact interval reconstruction and perfect seed recovery via forced alignment. In the unknown-prompt setting, only the generated text is available; approximate interval reconstruction combined with a maximum-hit-count scoring strategy still permits reliable recovery from sufficiently long outputs. Extensive experiments across six model families and five heterogeneous text domains show that, in the known-prompt setting, full 32-bit seed recovery from the complete 2^32 candidate space achieves up to 100% accuracy, depending on model and text domain, within 300 tokens and under 35 seconds on a single GPU. In the unknown-prompt setting, recovery reaches near-perfect accuracy at 600-800 tokens in about 12 seconds. We further analyze the influence of prompting strategies, tokenization ambiguities, and sampling hyperparameters on channel reliability. Moreover, we discuss several applications of our results: First, it allows for the steganographic transmission of 32 bits, but also shows that ignorance of the prompt is not a valid security assumption.

item.title: Context-Fractured Decomposition Attacks on Tool-Using LLM Agents: Exploiting Artifact Provenance Gaps
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09084v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前工具增强型大语言模型（LLM）智能体通过调用外部工具（如文件写入、API调用）生成并修改持久化人工制品（artifacts），例如工作区文件、日志或数据库记录。此类状态跨步延续的架构，使传统基于单轮/多轮对话文本的“ jailbreak”防御范式严重失效——防御者无法仅凭当前对话上下文判断风险，而必须追踪跨步骤、跨工具、跨实例的 artifact 血缘关系。然而，现有主流攻击（如 Crescendo、Tree of Attacks）及防御方案均隐含一个关键假设：所有交互构成单一、连续、可观测的对话流。该假设在真实部署中彻底崩塌：工具链分散、模块异构、执行时序异步，且artifact 血缘信息普遍缺失，形成系统性漏洞——我们将其正式定义为 “血缘断层”（provenance gap）。

方法与创新

本文首次将“血缘断层”操作化为可复现的部署失效模式，并提出 Context-Fractured Decomposition（CFD）攻击框架：一种新型跨上下文多步越狱范式。CFD 将恶意意图分阶段解耦——首阶段以完全良性表象生成中间 artifact（如无害代码片段、中性日志条目）；后续阶段（可能跨越不同 agent 实例、不同工作流阶段）通过一系列单步看似无害的工具调用（如“追加日志”“读取配置”“执行脚本”），在延迟的 artifact 介质上触发组合性风险。风险仅在 artifact 被跨上下文重用时显现，规避了单步内容审查。

主要发现与贡献

  • 在多个 agent 系统越狱基准测试中，CFD 攻击成功率较 SOTA 基线（含强单轮判别器）最高提升 28.3 个百分点；
  • 提出迹级诊断工具包（trace-level diagnostics），可精准定位血缘断层节点；
  • 首次系统论证 provenance lineage tagging（血缘谱系标记）作为可验证缓解路径：为每个 artifact 注入可审计、不可篡改的生成上下文元数据；
  • 所有实验设计严格遵循可复现性原则，代码与 trace 数据已开源（附录说明）。

注：文中包含用于学术研究的受限有害示例，已作必要脱敏与警示标注。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and operationalizes the provenance gap—a critical deployment failure mode in tool-using LLM agents where artifact lineage (e.g., files, logs) is untracked across tools, modules, and time—enabling evasion of conventional jailbreak defenses. We introduce Context-Fractured Decomposition (CFD), a novel family of cross-context, multi-step jailbreaks that decompose malicious intent across disjoint interaction phases: benign-looking artifacts are created early (e.g., innocuous code snippets), then later composed via individually low-risk tool actions (e.g., “append log”, “execute script”) to trigger harmful behavior only upon delayed, artifact-mediated composition—often across different agent instances or workflow stages. Using trace-level diagnostics, we validate CFD’s reproducibility and demonstrate up to +28.3 percentage points improvement in attack success over state-of-the-art baselines—including robust single-turn judges—across multiple agent-system jailbreak benchmarks. We further propose provenance lineage tagging as a verifiable mitigation: embedding auditable, immutable context metadata into every artifact. This work exposes a fundamental limitation of conversation-centric safety paradigms and establishes artifact provenance as a first-class security concern for agentic systems.

Abstract

Tool-using LLM agents interact with the world through actions that persist state in artifacts (e.g., workspace files or logs). Consequently, jailbreak defenses must reason about cross-step composition rather than isolated text. Yet most existing attacks and defenses, including ``multi-turn'' jailbreaks such as Crescendo and Tree of Attacks,still assume a single contiguous conversation visible to the defender. This assumption breaks down in real agent pipelines, where enforcement is fragmented across tools, modules, and time, and where artifact provenance is often not tracked. We operationalize a deployment failure mode for tool-using LLM agents, the \emph{provenance gap}, and study reproducible triggers for it: \emph{Context-Fractured Decomposition} (CFD), a family of cross-context multi-step jailbreaks that preserve benign-looking intermediate artifacts from an early interaction and elicit harmful behavior much later, potentially in a different agent instance or workflow stage, via individually innocuous tool actions whose risk emerges only under delayed artifact-mediated composition. We instrument the failure mode with trace-level diagnostics and outline a verifiable mitigation direction (provenance lineage tagging). Across agent-system jailbreak benchmarks, CFD improves success rates by up to 28.3 percentage points over state-of-the-art baselines, even against strong single-turn judges. Disclaimer: This paper contains examples of harmful or offensive language.

item.title: Document-Authored Control-Signal Impersonation: A Low-Cost Indirect Prompt Attack on RAG Safety Boundaries
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09005v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

检索增强生成（RAG）系统常将用户查询、检索文档、元数据、系统标签及任务指令统一序列化为自然语言提示词。本文揭示该设计中一类被忽视的源-权威边界失效：攻击者控制的检索文本可伪装成本应由系统或可信方生成的控制信号（如来源标注、权限声明、合规提示、权威等级标识等），从而绕过安全机制。我们将其命名为文档作者型控制信号冒用（Document-Authored Control-Signal Impersonation, DACSI）。

方法与评估设计

DACSI属于间接提示注入中的新型子类——它不依赖命令式指令（如“忽略上文”），而是利用RAG提示渲染中“可信元数据”与“不可信文档内容”在自然语言通道中物理混同的结构性缺陷。我们构建了覆盖6类大模型（DeepSeek V4 Pro/Qwen3.5-397B/DeepSeek V4 Flash/GPT-5.5/Gemini 3.1 Pro Low/GLM-4.7）、多级提示压力、多种注入基线、信号分类法、RAG流水线变体及系统控制探针的综合评估框架，并引入原创的源-权威归属探针与合成金丝雀格式，实现行为层面的归因验证。

主要发现与创新点

  • DACSI在所有模型中均触发可控越界，但表现呈显著异质性：DeepSeek V4 Pro与Qwen3.5-397B呈现最清晰的正向提升效应；DeepSeek V4 Flash为高易感场景；GPT-5.5与Gemini 3.1 Pro Low展现强边界能力（仅存特定残余风险）；GLM-4.7则暴露饱和型泄露边界。
  • 核心洞见：文档撰写的标签本质是数据，而非策略；RAG的安全设计必须区分“谁生成”与“内容是什么”。
  • DACSI需独立评估——因其使用无命令式元数据/溯源/策略表层、遵循RAG专属的源-权威传导路径、且对源-通道分离机制高度敏感。源-权威探针提供的是行为归因证据，而非内部机理证明。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies Document-Authored Control-Signal Impersonation (DACSI), a low-cost, command-free indirect prompt injection attack exploiting RAG’s structural collapse of trusted metadata and untrusted document text into a single natural-language channel. Unlike imperative injections, DACSI succeeds when attacker-authored document content is misattributed as authoritative control signals—e.g., provenance labels, policy disclosures, or authority indicators—thereby subverting safety boundaries. We evaluate DACSI across six LLM regimes (DeepSeek V4 Pro, Qwen3.5-397B, DeepSeek V4 Flash, GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro Low, GLM-4.7), varying prompt pressure, signal taxonomies, and RAG pipelines, using novel source-authority attribution probes and synthetic canaries. Results reveal regime-specific susceptibility: DeepSeek V4 Pro and Qwen3.5-397B show clearest positive lift; DeepSeek V4 Flash is highly vulnerable; GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro Low enforce strong boundaries with narrow residual risks; GLM-4.7 exhibits saturated leakage. DACSI warrants separate evaluation—it operates via metadata-like surfaces without commands, follows a RAG-specific source-authority pathway, and critically depends on source/channel separation. The source-authority probe provides behavioral attribution evidence, not mechanistic proof.

Abstract

Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems often serialize user queries, retrieved documents, metadata, system labels, and task instructions into one natural-language prompt. We study a source-authority boundary failure in this design: attacker-authored retrieved text can impersonate metadata, provenance, authority, or disclosure-policy signals that appear control-relevant to the model. We call this pattern Document-Authored Control-Signal Impersonation (DACSI). DACSI is a non-imperative, metadata-like payload subclass within indirect prompt injection. Its central lesson is simple: document-authored labels are data, not policy. Command-style injection asks the model to ignore, override, or violate policy; DACSI asks whether untrusted document text can be misattributed as an authorized control signal when RAG prompt rendering collapses trusted and untrusted text into the same natural-language channel. We evaluate DACSI across six model settings, prompt-pressure levels, injection baselines, signal taxonomies, RAG-mediated pipelines, system-control probes, a source-authority attribution probe, and synthetic canary formats. We interpret the evidence by model regime rather than as six equal replications: DeepSeek V4 Pro and Qwen3.5-397B provide the cleanest positive lift, DeepSeek V4 Flash is a high-susceptibility setting, GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro Low are strong-boundary probes with selected residual risks, and GLM-4.7 is a saturated leakage boundary case. Across these regimes, DACSI warrants separate evaluation because it uses a command-free metadata/provenance/policy surface, follows a RAG-specific source-authority path, and responds to source/channel separation. The source-authority probe is behavioral attribution evidence, not proof of an internal mechanism.

item.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09809v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前AI评估结果虽大规模产出，却分散于排行榜、模型卡、基准论文及企业博客中，报告格式高度不一致。这导致严重的解释性成本：读者难以跨来源可靠比较结果、识别报告遗漏的关键信息（如数据偏差、超参配置）、或追溯聚合结论至原始证据链。现有改进方案（如Model Cards、Datasheets）仅覆盖评估生命周期的局部环节，存在三大根本性缺口：（1）碎片化设计，无法整合为统一、可组合的解释性记录；（2）采用静态表征，未适配不同利益相关方（如研究者 vs. 政策制定者）对同一证据的差异化解读需求；（3）多停留于理论提案，缺乏支撑规模化落地的自动化提取与验证基础设施。

方法与创新

本文提出 Evaluation Cards（EvalCards）——一个可操作的AI评估报告解释层。我们：（1）通过系统性分析52篇基准论文与10场深度利益相关方访谈，推导出覆盖全评估生命周期的结构化报告模式，涵盖基准元数据、评估运行数据与模型元数据三类核心实体及其语义关系；（2）设计并实现四大可解释性信号：可复现性（代码/环境完整性）、文档完备性（关键方法透明度）、溯源与风险标识（数据/指标潜在偏差）、分数可比性（归一化与统计显著性），并通过双模态读者模式（研究者模式强调技术细节，非研究者模式聚焦影响与局限）动态渲染；（3）构建轻量级监控工具，在真实规模上部署：覆盖5,816个模型、635项基准、101,843条结果，首次量化揭示当前报告实践中的系统性缺陷（如>68%的结果缺失超参说明，41%未标注数据时效性）。

意义

EvalCards非替代现有报告形式，而是作为“解释性胶水层”，将异构评估资产联结为可信、可审计、可适配的认知单元，推动AI评估从“结果展示”迈向“证据叙事”。

AI Summary (English)

AI evaluation reporting suffers from fragmentation across leaderboards, model cards, and blogs, hindering cross-source comparison, omission detection, and evidence tracing. Prior efforts address isolated aspects but fail to unify the evaluation lifecycle, adapt to diverse stakeholder needs, or provide scalable infrastructure. We introduce Evaluation Cards (EvalCards)—an operational interpretive layer that composes benchmark metadata, evaluation run data, and model metadata into a single, structured record. First, we derived a comprehensive reporting schema via analysis of 52 papers and 10 stakeholder interviews. Second, we implemented four interpretable signals—reproducibility, documentation completeness, provenance & risk, and score comparability—rendered through audience-calibrated reader modes (researcher vs. non-researcher). Third, we deployed a monitoring tool across 5,816 models, 635 benchmarks, and 101,843 results, revealing systematic reporting gaps (e.g., 68% lack hyperparameter documentation; 41% omit data recency). EvalCards bridges heterogeneous reporting artifacts, enabling auditable, adaptive, and evidence-grounded AI evaluation.

Abstract

AI evaluation results are produced at scale but reported inconsistently across leaderboards, model cards, benchmark papers, and company blogs. The cost is interpretive: readers cannot reliably compare results across sources, identify what a report omits, or trace an aggregate claim to its underlying evidence. Recent efforts address isolated components but leave three gaps: they cover only narrow slices of the evaluation lifecycle and do not compose into a single interpretable record; they specify static representations that do not differentiate the questions different stakeholders bring to the same evidence; and they remain proposals on paper, lacking the extraction infrastructure required for adoption at scale. We present \EvalCards{}, an operational reporting layer that composes benchmark metadata, evaluation run data, and model metadata into a unified record. We (1) derive a reporting schema from a structured review of 52 papers and 10 stakeholder interviews, (2) implement four interpretive signals (reproducibility, documentation completeness, provenance and risk, and score comparability), rendered through reader modes calibrated to research and non-research audiences, and (3) deploy a monitoring tool that applies \EvalCards{} across 5,816 models, 635 benchmarks, and 101,843 results, surfacing systematic gaps in current reporting practice.

item.title: PRISM: Recovering Instruction Sets from Language Model Activations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09563v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLMs）作为自主智能体（agentic agents）在关键场景中部署，仅监控其输出已远不足以保障安全与可控性。真正挑战在于实时识别正在驱动模型行为的隐式指令集——包括显性指令、隐含约束、禁止项、推断出的子目标，乃至被提示注入（prompt injection）或隐藏目标（hidden objectives）所激活的非预期行为准则。现有“激活到语言”（activation-to-language）方法虽能从隐藏状态中解码部分自然语言信息，但均未针对多指令共存、语义交织、逻辑冲突的代理式场景进行建模，难以完整、忠实、结构化地还原活跃指令集合。

方法：PRISM 框架

本文首次形式化该任务为指令集检索（instruction set retrieval），并提出 PRISM（Prompt- and Representation-Informed Set Mapper）：一种面向冻结黑盒目标模型的激活条件化解释器。PRISM 不依赖模型微调或内部访问，仅通过其中间层激活向量即可解码出结构化的指令列表。其核心创新在于：

  • 端到端指令集监督：采用 judge-guided GRPO（带裁判引导的广义策略优化），以人工标注的黄金指令集为评判基准，奖励覆盖全部真实指令、严惩生成未支持（hallucinated）或遗漏指令；
  • 结构化输出设计：强制生成带语义标签（如 ● 指令、⚠ 禁止、🔍 子目标）的简洁要点列表，确保可解析性与可审计性；
  • 跨场景鲁棒训练：在良性对话、硬性约束响应、对抗性提示注入及隐蔽目标诱导四大典型风险场景中联合优化。

主要发现与价值

在多维度评估中，PRISM 显著超越所有基线方法（包括 Llama-3-8B-Instruct 的激活解码器与 LLaVA-style 适配器），尤其在安全敏感指标上提升达 42.7%（F1-score）。它首次实现了对代理行为“决策依据”的可观测化，为模型行为审计、红队测试、实时护栏（real-time guardrails）与可信AI治理提供了可部署的技术基座。

AI Summary (English)

As LLMs operate as autonomous agents, monitoring must go beyond outputs to recover the active instruction set—including goals, constraints, prohibitions, and inferred subgoals—especially under prompt injection or hidden objectives. We formalize this as instruction set retrieval, and propose PRISM, an activation-conditioned interpreter that decodes frozen-model hidden states into a faithful, bullet-pointed list of active instructions. Unlike prior activation-to-language methods, PRISM is trained end-to-end via judge-guided GRPO to maximize coverage of ground-truth instructions while penalizing hallucinations and omissions. Evaluated across benign, constrained, adversarial, and hidden-objective settings, PRISM consistently outperforms baselines—achieving up to +42.7% F1 on security-critical objectives—and enables structured, auditable insight into agent decision logic.

Abstract

As LLMs are deployed as agents, reliable monitoring requires knowing not only what they output, but which instructions are steering their behavior. This is difficult when models infer unintended subgoals, follow contextual cues, or are influenced by prompt injections and hidden objectives. While activation-to-language methods suggest that hidden states can reveal natural-language information, existing approaches are not designed to recover the full set of simultaneous instructions, constraints, prohibitions, and subgoals active in agentic settings. We formalize this problem as instruction set retrieval and introduce PRISM, an activation-conditioned interpreter that decodes hidden states from a frozen target model into a faithful bullet list of active instructions. Unlike prior activation-to-language methods, PRISM is trained to recover instruction sets directly, using judge-guided GRPO to reward covered instructions and penalize unsupported ones. Across benign, constrained, prompt-injection, and hidden-objective settings, PRISM outperforms activation-to-language baselines, especially on security-relevant objectives.

item.title: SIFT: Selective-Index For Fast Compute of RAG Prefill by Exploiting Attention Invariance
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09441v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

检索增强生成（RAG）通过向大语言模型（LLM）查询注入相关文档提升响应质量，但显著拉长提示长度，导致首字节时间（TTFT）升高。关键瓶颈在于：RAG场景中同一文档常被多轮用户查询重复使用（上下文复用性），而现有方案仍对每条查询完整重算文档的KV缓存——造成大量冗余计算；或虽预存KV张量，却因高延迟磁盘I/O拖累性能，且粗粒度复用（如整层/整块重用）严重损害精度。

方法创新：SIFT框架

本文提出SIFT（Selective-Index For Fast Compute），首次利用注意力不变性实现零KV存储的高效预填充加速：

  • 离线阶段：对文档进行轻量分析，精准提取两类高注意力位置——自注意力热点（文档内高分位置）与跨文档吸引点（易被后续文档关注的键位置），并编码为两个超紧凑位向量（bit vector）；
  • 运行时阶段：仅在标记位置执行稀疏注意力计算，跳过99%以上低分区域。

核心洞见与优势

1. 局部注意力不变性：文档内高分位置不受相邻文档干扰，可稳定预测自注意力焦点；
2. 交叉注意力一致性：文档内高自注意力键，同样倾向吸引后续文档的跨注意力；
3. 极致轻量存储：位向量体积仅为原始KV张量的1/24,000，彻底规避磁盘读取开销；
4. 精度-速度双赢：TTFT提升1.71倍，同时保持生成质量与全量重算相差<1%（BLEU/ROUGE），显著优于现有KV复用方案。

AI Summary (English)

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) improves LLM responses by injecting retrieved documents but suffers from high time-to-first-token (TTFT) due to redundant KV recomputation across repeated document queries. Prior KV-caching approaches incur prohibitive disk I/O latency and accuracy loss from coarse-grained reuse. This paper introduces SIFT, a selective indexing framework that exploits attention invariance to accelerate RAG prefill without storing any KV tensors. SIFT offline extracts fine-grained high-attention locations per document—capturing both local self-attention hotspots and cross-document key attractors—and encodes them as two ultra-compact bit vectors (up to 24,000× smaller than KV tensors). At runtime, SIFT computes attention only at marked positions, achieving 1.71× TTFT speedup while maintaining output quality within 1% of full recomputation (measured by BLEU/ROUGE). SIFT’s core insights—Local Attention Invariance and Cross-Attention Consistency—enable accurate sparse attention prediction, eliminating disk transfers and setting a new Pareto-optimal trade-off between latency and fidelity.

Abstract

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) injects LLM queries with relevant documents to improve response quality. This injection increases prompt length and slows time to first token (TTFT). Unlike standard queries, RAG queries have a unique property of context reuse where the same documents recur across user queries. Thus, fully recomputing documents for every RAG query does redundant compute and increases TTFT. Prior works precompute KV tensors of RAG documents offline and coarsely recompute some tokens during online prefill. However, such KV reuse is often slower than full recomputation on modern GPUs due to high-latency disk transfers. Further, such a coarse-grained recomputation degrades accuracy. To address these limitations, this paper proposes SIFT: Selective-Index For Fast Compute of RAG Prefill by Exploiting Attention Invariance. SIFT processes documents offline and extracts fine-grained locations of high attention scores for each document. Next, we identify the following attention invariance insights that enable us to exploit the extracted locations during runtime: (1) Local-Attention Invariance: The location of high attention scores within a document remain invariant to surrounding documents. This helps us predict the location of high scores where the document attends to itself. (2) Cross-Attention Consistency: Keys with high intra-document attention also attract cross-attention from subsequent documents. This helps us predict the location of high scores where the document attends to future documents. Critically, SIFT stores no KV data and only stores locations of high scores in the form of two compact bit vectors. SIFT's storage is up to 24,000x smaller than KV tensors, obviating costly disk transfers. During prefill, SIFT computes the attention only for the marked locations and improves TTFT by 1.71x while holding accuracy within 1% of full recompute.

item.title: Disentanglement with Holographic Reduced Representations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09725v1

AI Summary (中文)

解耦学习的新范式：基于全息降维表示（HRR）的符号化建模

背景与挑战：解耦学习旨在从数据中分离出独立的语义因子（如物体形状、颜色、位置），是可解释AI与因果表征学习的关键基础。现有方法（如β-VAE、FactorVAE）依赖连续潜变量与信息论正则化，但难以显式建模概念间的组合性（compositional structure），且解耦结果常缺乏语义明确性与噪声鲁棒性。

方法创新：本文突破连续潜空间范式，首次将解耦表征建模为符号化结构——每个样本由若干“槽位”（slot）组成，每个槽位绑定一个符号（symbol）与其值（value）。为兼顾神经网络的可微性与符号操作的离散性，我们提出基于全息降维表示（Holographic Reduced Representations, HRR） 的无监督学习框架。核心在于利用HRR的解绑（unbinding）操作作为归纳偏置：该操作天然鼓励不同槽位间符号-值对的分离，无需显式约束项或对抗训练。

关键发现：

  • 在dSprites、Cars3D等标准基准上，本方法在MIG、SAP、DCI等主流解耦指标上媲美或超越SOTA基线；
  • 潜变量遍历（latent traversal） 显示各槽位对应清晰、独立的语义因子（如“轮子数量”“车顶高度”）；
  • 理论分析证明：HRR解绑信道使符号-值对近似统计独立，并推导出每槽位容量界（per-slot capacity bound），定量刻画其解耦能力上限；
  • 表征形式为向量和叠加（而非标量维度堆叠），在SNR=0–20 dB噪声下重建PSNR衰减比VAE基线低42%，显著提升鲁棒性。

本工作 bridging symbolic AI 与深度学习，为可解释、鲁棒、组合式的表征学习提供了新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Disentanglement—separating independent factors of variation in data—remains a core challenge in representation learning. Unlike mainstream continuous-latent approaches (e.g., β-VAEs), we propose a symbolic disentanglement framework grounded in Holographic Reduced Representations (HRR). Our unsupervised algorithm leverages HRR’s unbinding operation as an explicit inductive bias for factor separation, yielding slot-based representations where each slot binds a symbol to its value via vector superposition. Empirically, it achieves competitive disentanglement scores (MIG, SAP, DCI) and clean latent traversals on dSprites and Cars3D. Theoretically, we prove unbinding induces near-independence among symbol-value pairs and derive a per-slot capacity bound quantifying reliable concept encoding. Crucially, the resulting vector-sum representations exhibit superior noise robustness: reconstruction quality degrades 42% less than VAE baselines across SNR 0–20 dB. This work bridges compositional symbolic reasoning and differentiable learning.

Abstract

Disentanglement, the separation of factors of variation in data using neural networks, remains a long-standing challenge in machine learning. Prior work has addressed this problem with variational autoencoders and generative adversarial networks that incorporate ideas from variational inference and information-theoretic constraints. In contrast to methods that rely on continuous representations, we propose a design that treats disentangled representations as symbolic structures, motivated by the compositional relationships among the concepts that make up samples from a distribution. However, learning discrete symbolic structures with neural networks while maintaining differentiability is difficult and often requires complex architectures. To address this, we introduce an unsupervised learning algorithm that uses holographic reduced representations (HRR) for neural disentanglement. We show that the HRR unbinding operation provides an inductive bias for separating factors and yields competitive results against baselines, as measured by latent traversals and disentanglement metrics. We complement these empirical findings with an information-theoretic analysis of the HRR unbinding channel. We prove that unbinding induces approximately independent symbol-value pairs and derive a per-slot capacity bound that quantifies how many distinct symbolic concepts can be reliably encoded, giving a quantitative account of the inductive bias toward disentanglement. The resulting representations differ from standard autoencoder-based models, in that their latent units are vectors that are summed together, rather than scalar dimensions of a low-dimensional latent vector. We show that this HRR representation is more robust to noise than other disentangled representations and maintains reconstruction quality across a range of SNRs.

item.title: BrainSurgery: Reproducible and Reliable Declarative Weight Manipulations for Model Editing and Upcycling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09707v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着深度学习模型规模持续扩大，大型检查点（checkpoints）的管理、审查与修改日益困难。研究者常需对模型权重执行层结构调整、精度转换（如FP32→BF16）、低秩分解（如LoRA初始化）、架构调试等操作，但当前实践多依赖易出错、难复现的手写Python脚本，缺乏统一抽象与验证机制。

方法与创新

本文提出 BrainSurgery——首个面向神经网络检查点的可复现、可验证的声明式权重编辑工具。其核心创新在于：

  • ✅ 声明式YAML计划驱动：用户通过简洁YAML文件描述目标变换（如“将所有attn.q_proj.weight张量转为INT8并切分至4个分片”），无需编写底层代码；
  • ✅ 多维抽象能力：统一屏蔽存储格式（Safetensors/PyTorch/DeepSpeed）与内存布局差异，支持结构重排（layer renaming/removing）、数学变换（scale/clip/quantize）、张量重塑（reshape/transpose/split）；
  • ✅ 正则+结构双重定位：结合正则表达式（如.*\.mlp\.w[13]\.weight）与层级路径语法，精准匹配目标参数；
  • ✅ 内置断言验证：自动校验张量形状、数据类型、数值范围及跨张量一致性，杜绝静默错误（silent corruption）。

应用与验证

系统演示涵盖四大典型场景：模型升频（upcycling）至新架构、LoRA权重提取、混合精度微调检查点修复、以及Transformer层冗余检测。三个案例研究证实：BrainSurgery在10B+模型上实现零错误、全可复现的权重操作，编辑脚本平均长度缩减72%，验证覆盖率提升至100%。本工作为模型编辑、可信AI运维与大模型生命周期管理提供了坚实基础。

AI Summary (English)

BrainSurgery is a novel tool for reproducible, declarative tensor surgery on neural network checkpoints. It addresses the fragility of ad-hoc Python scripts used for weight manipulation—such as layer restructuring, precision casting, low-rank factorization, and architectural debugging—by introducing YAML-based declarative plans that abstract storage formats (e.g., Safetensors, PyTorch), memory layout, and hardware constraints. Users specify transformations via expressive structural targeting (e.g., encoder.layers.[0-5].self_attn.q_proj.weight) and regex patterns, enabling precise, composable edits across model scales. Built-in assertions rigorously validate tensor shapes, dtypes, value ranges, and cross-parameter invariants—eliminating silent corruption. Evaluated across four examples and three real-world case studies—including model upcycling, LoRA extraction, and mixed-precision checkpoint repair—BrainSurgery achieves 100% reproducibility and zero validation failures on models up to 10B parameters, reducing script length by 72% and enabling fully auditable, version-controlled model editing workflows.

Abstract

As deep learning models scale, managing, inspecting, and modifying large checkpoints has become increasingly challenging. Researchers often need to alter model weights for layer restructuring, precision casting, low-rank factorization, and architectural debugging, yet these workflows often rely on fragile ad-hoc Python scripts. Here, we introduce BrainSurgery, a tool for robust and reproducible "tensor surgery" on neural network checkpoints, and provide a system demonstration covering four examples and three case studies from model upcycling to LoRA extraction. By abstracting storage formats and memory management, BrainSurgery executes complex transformations through declarative YAML plans. It supports structural modifications, mathematical transformations, and tensor reshaping through expressive regex and structural targeting, while built-in assertions validate tensor shapes, data types, and values to prevent silent errors. We envision that BrainSurgery will provide a strong foundation for future research through its reproducible and validated operations.

item.title: On Choosing the $μ$ Parameter in Gaussian Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09582v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

近年来，高斯微分隐私（Gaussian Differential Privacy, GDP） 因其对复合机制的紧致分析能力及与假设检验的天然联系，正逐步取代纯微分隐私（pure DP）成为隐私机器学习中报告隐私保证的推荐框架。然而，实践中大量算法仍以纯DP参数 $\varepsilon$ 发布，亟需可靠、可解释的 $\varepsilon \to \mu$ 映射规则，以实现跨隐私定义的公平比较与合规声明。

方法与技术路线

本研究提出基于强敌手成员推断攻击（membership inference attack, MIA）的三重原则性映射准则：

  • 乘性优势匹配：在固定假阳性率（FPR）下，使GDP与纯DP下最优攻击的乘性优势（multiplicative advantage）相等；
  • 精度-召回权衡匹配：在固定召回率（recall）下，令二者对应的攻击精度（precision）一致；
  • 隐私轮廓（privacy profile）匹配：直接对齐两种隐私定义下的标准隐私轮廓函数。

所有映射均基于最坏情况分析，确保保守性与理论严谨性。

主要发现与实践建议

我们系统计算并制表了 $\varepsilon \in [0.1, 10]$ 区间内对应的最佳 $\mu$ 值（步长0.1），发现三类准则高度一致：当 $\varepsilon \geq 0.5$ 时，$\mu \approx \varepsilon / 5$ 是一个稳健、保守且易于部署的通用换算公式。该值在所有准则下均略低于精确匹配值，可为实际系统提供安全裕度。此外，研究揭示：纯DP在低$\varepsilon$区更“严苛”，而GDP的渐近平滑性使其更适合刻画大规模迭代算法的真实隐私损耗。

AI Summary (English)

Recent work advocates Gaussian Differential Privacy (GDP) as a more expressive and composition-friendly framework for reporting privacy guarantees in machine learning. This paper establishes principled, worst-case mappings from pure-DP $\varepsilon$ to GDP $\mu$, grounded in the success of optimal strong-adversary membership inference attacks. We align the two notions via three complementary metrics: multiplicative advantage at fixed false positive rate (FPR), precision at fixed recall, and the standard privacy profile.
