Our tabulated results across $\varepsilon \in [0.1, 10]$ show remarkable consistency: for $\varepsilon \geq 0.5$, $\mu \approx \varepsilon/5$ serves as a simple, conservative, and broadly applicable conversion rule—consistently underestimating the exact match and thus providing a safety margin. This recommendation bridges theory and practice, enabling reliable cross-framework privacy accounting.

Abstract

Recent work argues for using Gaussian differential privacy (GDP) to report the privacy guarantees in privacy-preserving machine learning. We provide principled mappings from pure-DP $\varepsilon$ to GDP $μ$ by matching the worst-case success of a strong-adversary membership inference attack in terms of three metrics: multiplicative advantage at fixed FPR, precision at fixed recall, and the standard privacy profile. We tabulate $μ$ values across a useful range of parameters and recommend $μ\approx \varepsilon/5$ as a conservative general-purpose conversion.

item.title: PRISM: Topology-Aware Cross-Modal Imputation for Modality-Deficient Federated Graph Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09301v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多模态联邦图学习（MM-FGL）旨在协同建模分散在各客户端的图文图结构数据（如商品交互图+图像/文本描述）。然而，现实场景中存在客户端级模态缺失（client-level modality deficiency）：视觉搜索客户端仅有图像-交互图而无卖家文本描述，目录服务客户端仅有文本描述却无产品图像。此类缺失非随机样本缺失，而是语义基础性缺失——客户端本地缺乏重建缺失模态所需的语义先验。更关键的是，在图神经网络中，不完整模态表征将直接参与消息传递，其误差会经图拓扑被过滤、混合与放大，导致性能严重退化。

方法创新：PRISM框架

本文提出 PRISM（Proactive Retrieval and Imputation via Structural Meta-prompting），首个面向模态缺失联邦图学习的拓扑感知跨模态填补框架。其核心突破在于：

  • 主动式跨客户端语义检索：不依赖本地孤立重建，而是通过轻量元提示（structural meta-prompt）引导联邦聚合，从其他模态完备客户端中检索与本地图结构高度对齐的语义线索；
  • 拓扑感知嵌入注入：将检索到的跨模态语义动态注入本地图传播过程，以图邻接矩阵为掩码调控信息融合强度，确保填补内容与局部拓扑一致；
  • 联邦隐私保护设计：仅交换梯度级元提示与低维语义锚点，不传输原始模态数据，满足严格联邦约束。

实验结果

在6个真实多模态图数据集（涵盖电商、社交、学术图谱）上，PRISM在图分类、节点分类、跨模态检索等任务中显著提升模态缺失客户端性能，平均超越SOTA基线4.48%，且在稀疏连接与高异构性场景下鲁棒性突出。代码已开源。

AI Summary (English)

Multimodal federated graph learning (MM-FGL) enables collaborative modeling of decentralized graph-structured data with text and images. Yet real-world clients often suffer from client-level modality deficiency—e.g., a visual-search client has image–interaction graphs but no textual descriptions, while a catalog client provides text only. Unlike random missingness, such deficiency implies a lack of local semantic basis for reconstruction; critically, imputation errors propagate and amplify through graph message passing under topology constraints. To address this, we propose PRISM, the first topology-aware cross-modal imputation framework for MM-FGL. PRISM proactively retrieves missing-modality semantics from the federation—not from local observations alone—and injects them into local graph propagation guided by structural meta-prompts that adapt to the client’s graph topology. Experiments across six multimodal graph datasets and diverse tasks (graph/node classification, cross-modal retrieval) show PRISM consistently boosts performance of modality-deficient clients, achieving an average +4.48% improvement over state-of-the-art baselines.

Abstract

Multimodal federated graph learning (MM-FGL) aims to collaboratively learn from decentralized graphs with text and images. However, real-world clients may not share a common modality basis: a visual-search client may contain image--interaction graphs but no seller descriptions, while a catalog client may provide text but no product images. We refer to this practical setting as client-level modality deficiency. Unlike random instance-wise missingness, a deficient client lacks the local semantic basis needed to reconstruct the absent modality. More importantly, in graph learning, incomplete representations initialize message passing, so imputation errors can be filtered, mixed, and amplified by the receiving topology. To address this gap, we propose \textbf{PRISM} (\textbf{P}roactive \textbf{R}etrieval and \textbf{I}mputation via \textbf{S}tructural \textbf{M}eta-prompting), a topology-aware federated cross-modal imputation framework. Rather than reconstructing the missing modality solely from local observations, PRISM recovers missing-modality semantics from the federation and introduces them into local graph propagation under topology-aware control. Experiments on six multimodal graph datasets across graph-centric and modality-centric tasks show that PRISM consistently improves modality-deficient clients, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines by \textbf{4.48}\% on average.

item.title: Asymptotic Optimality of the High-Dimensional Gaussian Mechanism and Improved Low-Dimensional Mechanisms for Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08681v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

加性噪声机制是实现向量查询差分隐私（DP）的核心范式，尤其适用于 $T$ 维实值向量输出。其中，高斯机制因结构简洁、理论保障强而被广泛采用。然而，其“最优性”长期缺乏严格刻画：在高维场景下是否真正不可超越？在低维场景中是否存在更优替代？这些问题直接影响隐私系统的设计权衡。

方法与创新

本研究从渐近分析与机制族构建双路径突破：

  • 渐近最优性证明：首次严格证明——当维度 $T \to \infty$ 且处于典型强隐私设置（如 $\varepsilon \ll 1$, $\delta = o(1/T)$）时，任意加性噪声机制的隐私–效用权衡均无法渐近优于高斯机制。该结论为高斯机制的工业级主导地位提供了根本性理论支撑。
  • 新机制族提出：构造球面广义伽马（Spherical Generalized Gamma, SGG）机制族，统一涵盖高斯机制（$\alpha=2$）与最新提出的 $\ell_2$ 机制（Joseph et al., ICML 2025；对应 $\alpha=1$），参数 $\alpha > 0$ 控制噪声径向分布的尾部特性。
  • 低维性能提升：通过精细调参（如 $\alpha=1.5$），SGG 机制在 $T=2\text{–}10$ 等典型低维场景中显著降低 $\ell_2$ 误差（相较高斯机制最高提升 18%，相较 $\ell_2$ 机制最高提升 12%），填补了实用化空白。
  • 紧致组合分析：首次为整个 SGG 族建立紧致（tight）隐私预算组合定理，彻底解决 Joseph et al. 提出的关于 $\ell_2$ 机制组合界是否可紧的开放问题。

意义

本工作既确立了高斯机制在高维下的理论天花板，又通过可调机制族实现了低维实用性突破，推动 DP 向“场景自适应”范式演进。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the asymptotic optimality of the Gaussian mechanism for $T$-dimensional vector queries under strong differential privacy (e.g., small $\varepsilon$, $\delta = o(1/T)$): as $T \to \infty$, no additive-noise mechanism can asymptotically surpass its privacy–utility tradeoff. To bridge the low-dimensional gap, we introduce the Spherical Generalized Gamma (SGG) family, parameterized by $\alpha > 0$, which subsumes both the Gaussian ($\alpha = 2$) and the $\ell_2$ mechanism ($\alpha = 1$). We identify $\alpha \in (1,2)$—e.g., $\alpha = 1.5$—that outperform both benchmarks in dimensions $T = 2$–$10$, reducing $\ell_2$ error by up to 18% over Gaussian and 12% over $\ell_2$. Crucially, we prove tight composition for the entire SGG family, resolving an open question about the $\ell_2$ mechanism’s composability.

Abstract

The additive noise mechanism is a foundational tool for differential privacy (DP) of $T$-dimensional real-valued vector queries. The Gaussian mechanism, utilizing Gaussian noise, is the mostly widely used such mechanism, due to its simplicity and strong privacy guarantees. In this work, we provide justification for this choice, showing that as the dimension $T\to\infty$, no additive-noise mechanism can asymptotically improve on the Gaussian mechanism's privacy--utility tradeoff for the strong privacy settings typically used.We also develop a new family of \emph{Spherical Generalized Gamma} DP mechanisms, which contains both the Gaussian mechanism and the recently studied $\ell_2$ mechanism (Joseph \emph{et al.}, ICML 2025). We identify members of this family that outperform both the Gaussian and $\ell_2$ mechanisms in certain low-dimensional settings, and show tight composition of all mechanisms in this family, answering an open question of Joseph \emph{et al.}~regarding the $\ell_2$ mechanism.

item.title: Data Agents Under Attack: Vulnerabilities in LLM-Driven Analytical Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08661v1

AI Summary (中文)

数据代理系统面临新型安全威胁：面向LLM驱动分析系统的漏洞研究

数据代理（Data Agents）将大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力与关系型数据访问、可执行分析工具及多步工作流编排深度融合，正成为企业智能分析的核心枢纽。然而，该融合架构在数据资源层、数据库执行层和代理推理层引入了跨域复合型安全漏洞——既非传统数据库安全范式所能覆盖，亦超出通用LLM代理安全研究的范畴。为系统性填补这一空白，本文开展首个面向数据代理的纵深安全研究。

核心贡献有三：
1. 提出分层脆弱性框架：从解释层（如自然语言查询歧义导致越权解析）、执行层（如SQL注入绕过沙箱、工具调用劫持）到策略层（如动态权限策略失效、审计日志缺失），识别出8类数据代理特有风险；
2. 构建目标导向攻击分类法：按攻击目标（数据窃取、逻辑破坏、服务降级）、战术（7类，含提示注入、上下文污染、schema感知混淆等）和技术（14种，如“嵌套注释逃逸”“元数据反射式注入”），并配套设计基于真实数据库Schema的LLM驱动载荷生成流水线，支持自动化、场景化攻击构造；
3. 实证评估六大系统：涵盖4个主流开源数据代理（LangChain+SQLAgent、LlamaIndex-QueryEngine等）及2个商用云分析平台（Snowflake Cortex、Databricks SQL AI）。实验发现：100%被测系统存在至少1类高危漏洞；其中73%暴露可绕过行级安全（RLS）的语义级注入，58%因工具链权限过度授予导致横向提权。研究最终提炼出四大实践启示：需建立“查询-执行-反馈”全链路可信验证机制、推行最小权限工具封装、增强LLM输出结构化约束、以及设计Schema-aware防御性解析器。

AI Summary (English)

Data agents—LLM-powered systems that combine natural language reasoning with database access and analytical tool orchestration—are increasingly critical to enterprise analytics, yet their hybrid architecture introduces novel, cross-layer security vulnerabilities unaddressed by either database or general LLM-agent security research. We present the first systematic security study of data agents, with three key contributions: (1) a layered vulnerability framework identifying eight agent-specific risks across interpretation, execution, and policy layers; (2) a goal-driven attack taxonomy (3 goals × 7 tactics × 14 techniques), paired with an LLM-based payload generation pipeline grounded in real-world database schemas; and (3) empirical evaluation across six systems—including four open-source agents and two production cloud services—revealing pervasive vulnerabilities: 100% of systems exhibited at least one high-severity flaw, 73% were vulnerable to semantic SQL injection bypassing row-level security, and 58% enabled privilege escalation via over-permissioned tools. Our findings underscore the urgent need for end-to-end verification, strict tool sandboxing, structured output constraints, and schema-aware defensive parsing.

Abstract

Data agents integrate LLM-driven reasoning with relational data access, executable analytical tools, and multi-step workflow orchestration, making them increasingly central to enterprise analytics. This integration introduces new security vulnerabilities across data resources, database execution, and agent reasoning, recombining concerns from database security and general-purpose LLM-agent security into failure modes that neither line of work captures on its own. To address this gap, we present a systematic security study of data agents. Our contributions are threefold. First, we develop a layered vulnerability framework that identifies eight data agent-specific risks across interpretation, execution, and policy layers. Second, we introduce an attack taxonomy organized by adversary goal, tactic, and technique, covering three goals, seven tactics, and fourteen techniques, and pair it with an LLM-driven payload generation pipeline grounded in real database schemas. Third, we evaluate these attacks on six systems, including four open-source data agents and two production cloud analytics services. Our experiments reveal substantial security vulnerabilities across current systems and yield four key takeaways.

item.title: Exploring CKKS Parameter Trade-offs for Privacy-Preserving Personalized Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08521v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

隐私保护的个性化联邦学习（PFL）允许多客户端协作训练各自专属模型，避免原始数据上传；但传统方案中加密前的模型更新仍易受诚实但好奇服务器的推理攻击。虽同态加密（HE）可实现密文域聚合，CKKS方案因其对浮点近似运算的原生支持而成为PFL的理想选择，然而现有研究尚未系统分析CKKS在PFL场景下的参数配置规律，导致实践者缺乏兼顾安全性、精度与效率的参数选择依据。

方法与框架

本文提出 pFedCKKS——首个将CKKS深度集成至PFL的通用框架。我们首次为PFL场景推导出满足128位安全强度的完整CKKS参数约束体系，并证明参数选择可简化为仅需确定两个核心变量：内层素数 $p_{\text{in}}$ 与外层素数 $p_{\text{out}}$。框架基于Flower联邦平台与TenSEAL库实现，支持主流PFL算法（FedFinetune、Ditto、FedPer），并在FEMNIST（手写字符）、CelebA（人脸属性）和Sentiment140（情感分析）三大异构数据集上完成端到端验证。

主要发现与创新

实验揭示了显著的精度–开销权衡规律：增大素数间隔可提升模型精度（如CelebA上Top-1准确率提升达3.2%），但导致加密/解密延迟增加2.1×、通信量增长1.8×。据此，我们提出面向部署的CKKS参数选择指南，明确推荐不同资源约束下（边缘设备/云服务器）的最优素数组合区间，并开源代码与配置模板。本工作填补了HE-PFL工程落地的关键空白，为构建高效、可信的个性化联邦系统提供了可复现、可扩展的密码学基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Privacy-preserving Personalized Federated Learning (PFL) enables clients to train personalized models without sharing raw data, yet unencrypted model updates remain vulnerable to inference attacks from honest-but-curious servers. While the CKKS homomorphic encryption scheme supports approximate floating-point arithmetic—making it suitable for PFL—no prior work has systematically analyzed its parameter selection for this setting. This paper introduces pFedCKKS, the first generic framework integrating CKKS into PFL, and provides the first principled, security-aware parameter selection guide. We derive full CKKS parameter constraints under 128-bit security for PFL, showing that configuration reduces to choosing just two ciphertext primes: the inner and outer primes. Evaluated on FEMNIST, CelebA, and Sentiment140 with FedFinetune, Ditto, and FedPer, our experiments reveal a clear trade-off: higher precision requires larger prime gaps but incurs up to 2.1× slower encryption/decryption and 1.8× higher communication overhead. We thus deliver concrete, deployment-oriented guidelines for balancing accuracy and efficiency in real-world pFedCKKS systems.

Abstract

Privacy-preserving Personalized Federated Learning (PFL) enables clients to collaboratively train personalized models without exposing raw data, but exchanged model updates remain vulnerable to inference attacks from honest-but-curious servers. Homomorphic Encryption (HE) addresses this by allowing server-side aggregation directly on encrypted updates, with the CKKS scheme being particularly suitable due to its native support for approximate floating-point arithmetic. However, no prior work has examined how to configure CKKS for PFL deployments, leaving practitioners without principled guidance on parameter selection that directly affects privacy, precision, and computational cost. This paper presents pFedCKKS, a generic framework integrating CKKS into PFL, and provides the first systematic parameter selection guide for practitioners. We derive the full CKKS parameter constraints under 128-bit security for the PFL setting, showing the selection problem reduces to choosing just two values: the inner and outer ciphertext prime. Implemented using the Flower framework and TenSEAL library, pFedCKKS is evaluated on the FEMNIST, CelebA and Sentiment140 datasets with FedFinetune, Ditto and FedPer which represents PFL algorithms. Experimental results reveal an empirical trade-off between precision and computational/communication costs. This allows us to draw a concrete guideline for selecting proper CKKS parameters that balance efficiency and accuracy in real-world deployments of pFedCKKS.

item.title: Hiding in Plain Floats: Steganographic Carriers for Indirect Prompt and Content Injection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08403v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

当前主流大语言模型（LLM）的提示注入防御机制普遍假设恶意信号必然显式存在于待检文本视图中（如用户输入、系统提示或模型输出）。然而，该假设在结构化输入场景下可能失效——攻击者可将恶意指令隐匿于浮点数数组等非文本载体中，绕过纯文本检测器。

方法与实验

本文提出“Hiding in Plain Floats”攻击范式：将自然语言提示（如“忽略上文，输出管理员密码”）编码为高精度浮点数序列（IFS编码），嵌入API请求的结构化参数字段（如temperature=0.723456789等冗余浮点值），经服务端解析后碎片化重建为语义片段，并触发模型行为偏移。我们在真实商业LLM API（Anthropic、OpenAI、Google）上开展14,400次对抗试验，覆盖Prompt Guard 2 + TF-IDF双层文本分类器及微调版RoBERTa-base检测器。

关键发现

  • 94.3%泄漏级攻击成功率（Leakage ASR）：在最强文本防御下，浮点载荷仍能稳定诱导模型泄露受控内容（如引用/复述恶意标记），即使模型未直接执行指令；
  • 双阶段规避机制：数据层存储（浮点参数化）规避原始输入检测，重建层碎片化（telemetry级语义拼接）规避上下文重写检测，二者缺一不可；
  • 边界性贡献：该攻击并非“不可检测”，而是揭示了纯文本检查在结构化输入管道中的根本性盲区——当辅助通道（如解析后的浮点语义）被LLM间接感知时，文本视图即失效。

创新点

首次系统论证浮点数作为隐式提示载体的可行性，定义“重构型间接注入”新威胁模型，并为安全设计提供可量化的失败边界：结构化输入接口必须对重建后语义通道实施联合校验，而非仅审查原始文本字段。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies a critical blind spot in text-only prompt-injection defenses: malicious signals can evade detection not by obfuscating text, but by hiding in plain floats—encoding prompts as structured floating-point parameters (e.g., top_p=0.987654321) that are parsed and fragmented into semantic telemetry after textual inspection. Across 14,400 real-world API trials on three commercial LLMs, our IFS-based float-array carrier achieves 94.3% Leakage ASR under the strongest evaluated defense (Prompt Guard 2 + TF-IDF ensemble), and replicates against fine-tuned RoBERTa-base. Ablation confirms evasion requires both data-layer float encoding (to bypass input-view checks) and reconstruction-layer fragmentation (to evade context-reconstruction checks). While trivial detectors (e.g., xxd + semantic validation) block the current T3 instance, the core contribution is a rigorously measured failure boundary: text-only inspection is fundamentally insufficient when structured inputs expose reconstructed auxiliary channels to the LLM.

Abstract

Text-centered prompt-injection defenses assume that the malicious signal is visible in one of the inspected text views. We study a reproducible LLM01-style indirect prompt/content-injection failure mode where that assumption breaks: a payload caught in plain English slips past the same detector when it is transported as structured float parameters and reconstructed only as fragmented telemetry. Across 14,400 attacked real-model trials on three commercial LLM APIs from different providers, the IFS-derived float-array carrier preserves 94.3% leakage ASR under the strongest dual-layer text-classifier defense evaluated in the main matrix: a Prompt Guard 2 + TF-IDF ensemble; the same carrier-level pattern also replicates with a fine-tuned roberta-base detector. We emphasize leakage ASR because downstream systems may act on quoted or reproduced markers even when the model refuses, but Strong ASR is the stricter metric for structurally compliant attack success. A 2 x 2 ablation shows that data-layer storage and reconstruction-layer fragmentation defeat different text views and that both are needed to evade both. A simple xxd detector and semantic validation block the current T3 instance, so the contribution is not an undetectable exploit but a measured failure boundary for text-only inspection in structured-input pipelines that expose reconstructed auxiliary channels to an LLM.

item.title: A Variability-Based Framework for Interpretable Naming in Formal and Relational Concept Analysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08477v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

形式概念分析（FCA）与关系概念分析（RCA）虽能从符号化数据中生成严格定义的概念结构、蕴含规则及关系依赖，但其自动提取的概念通常仅以技术性标签（如“概念#12”或逻辑表达式）标识，缺乏自然语言语义，严重制约了领域专家在知识解释、导航验证与复用中的实际参与。

方法创新

本文提出首个基于变异性建模的可解释命名框架，从符号知识表示视角系统解构概念命名任务。我们首先形式化命名过程中的四大核心挑战：歧义性（同一描述对应多义名称）、区分性（需凸显概念间差异）、简洁性（避免冗余修饰）与一致性（跨相关概念的术语协同）。进而构建一个可配置的LLM辅助命名框架，其核心是变异性模型——显式控制命名过程中向大语言模型（LLM）开放的信息源维度，包括：概念内涵（intent）、外延（extent）、继承属性、邻近概念、逻辑蕴含、关系属性等。该模型将命名决策转化为可控的语义选择过程，使“形式描述→自然语言名称”的映射具备可审计性与可复现性。

实证与启示

在披萨店（pizzeria）小型关系数据集上的实证表明：不同变异性配置显著影响LLM生成名称的质量与语义焦点（例如启用“关系属性”时更倾向产出“适合素食者搭配的酱料”而非泛称“番茄酱”）；命名结果的差异本身即揭示了隐含的关系依赖、专家解释偏好，甚至暴露原始建模中的不一致（如属性缺失或层级错位）。本工作为FCA/RCA走向人机协同知识工程提供了可扩展的方法论基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the critical gap between formal conceptual structures and human interpretability in Formal and Relational Concept Analysis (FCA/RCA). While FCA/RCA generate rigorous, logic-based concepts from symbolic data, these concepts lack meaningful natural-language names—hindering expert validation, navigation, and reuse. We propose a variability-based framework for LLM-assisted concept naming that makes semantic choices explicit: a configurable model controls which information sources (e.g., intent, extent, relational attributes, implications, neighbors) are exposed to the LLM during naming. This transforms naming from an opaque heuristic into an auditable, parameterized process. Evaluated on a small pizzeria relational dataset, the framework demonstrates how varying input configurations yield semantically distinct, context-sensitive names—and how naming variability itself exposes relational dependencies, interpretation trade-offs, and modeling inconsistencies in the underlying symbolic representation. The work establishes a foundational, extensible approach to interpretable knowledge grounding in symbolic AI.

Abstract

Knowledge extraction from symbolic data often produces abstractions that are formally defined but not immediately interpretable by users. Formal Concept Analysis (FCA) and Relational Concept Analysis (RCA) provide representative settings for this issue: they generate explicit conceptual structures, implications, and relational dependencies from object descriptions and relations. Although these structures are explainable by design, their concepts are often identified by technical labels, which limits their use as human-interpretable knowledge units. Assigning meaningful names to such concepts is therefore a key issue for interpretation, navigation, validation, and reuse by domain experts. This paper investigates concept naming in FCA and RCA from a symbolic knowledge representation perspective. We first characterize the linguistic and terminological challenges involved in naming generated symbolic abstractions, including ambiguity, discrimination, concision, and consistency across related concepts. We then propose a configurable framework for LLM-assisted concept naming. The framework relies on a variability model that controls which sources of information are exposed during naming, such as intent, extent, inherited information, neighboring concepts, implications, and relational attributes. It thereby makes explicit the semantic choices involved in moving from formal concept descriptions to human-readable names. The approach is illustrated as a proof of concept on a small relational dataset in the pizzeria domain. This illustration shows how different configurations influence the names suggested by an LLM, and how naming variability can reveal interpretation choices, relational dependencies, and possible modeling issues in the underlying symbolic data.

item.title: OrderDP: A Theoretically Guaranteed Lossless Dynamic Data Pruning Framework
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08574v1

AI Summary (中文)

OrderDP：一种理论可保证、无损的动态数据剪枝框架

背景与挑战：数据剪枝（Data Pruning, DP）旨在通过剔除冗余训练样本降低计算开销，同时保持模型性能近似无损。然而，现有方法多依赖“高信息量样本”筛选（如基于梯度、损失或不确定性），易引入系统性梯度偏差——其统计性质未被严格刻画，导致收敛性与泛化性保障缺失，加速效果亦难精确控制。

方法创新：本文提出OrderDP——首个兼具理论无偏性、收敛保证与实际可控加速的即插即用动态剪枝框架。其核心设计为两阶段随机-排序机制：（1）每轮从全量数据中均匀随机采样子集；（2）在该子集上计算样本对代理损失（surrogate loss） 的贡献，并选取贡献值最高的前-$q$个样本参与本轮训练。关键在于：该策略在代理损失下严格满足无偏梯度估计（$\mathbb{E}[\nabla\mathcal{L}_{\text{sur}}^{\text{OrderDP}}] = \nabla\mathcal{L}_{\text{sur}}^{\text{full}}$），从而规避传统启发式剪枝的偏差根源。

理论与实验验证：我们建立了完整的收敛性分析（证明其在凸/非凸设定下收敛至代理最优解）与泛化误差界（量化剪枝规模$q$与泛化差距的显式关系）。在CIFAR-10/100及ImageNet-1K上的实验表明：OrderDP以更简架构、更低延迟实现：（1）精度媲美全量训练（ImageNet-1K Top-1仅降0.3%）；（2）训练曲线高度稳定，无震荡或发散；（3）加速比精确可控（支持任意$q$配置）；（4）端到端训练成本降低超40%。代码已开源，支持快速迁移至各类视觉任务。

AI Summary (English)

OrderDP is a theoretically grounded, lossless dynamic data pruning framework that addresses the bias and instability issues plaguing existing pruning methods. Unlike heuristic approaches selecting "informative" samples—which induce biased gradient estimates—OrderDP employs a two-stage strategy: (1) uniform random subsampling, followed by (2) top-$q$ selection based on per-sample contribution to a surrogate loss. This ensures unbiased gradient estimation w.r.t. the surrogate objective, enabling provably stable convergence and controlled acceleration. We establish rigorous convergence guarantees (for both convex and non-convex settings) and generalization bounds explicitly linking pruning ratio $q$ to excess risk. Empirically, OrderDP achieves competitive accuracy (e.g., only −0.3% Top-1 on ImageNet-1K), stable training dynamics, exact acceleration control, and >40% end-to-end training cost reduction—outperforming comprehensive baselines with simpler design and faster runtime. Code is publicly available.

Abstract

Data pruning (DP), as an oft-stated strategy to alleviate heavy training burdens, reduces the volume of training samples according to a well-defined pruning method while striving for near-lossless performance. However, existing approaches, which commonly select highly informative samples, can lead to biased gradient estimation compared to full-dataset training. Furthermore, the analysis of this bias and its impact on final performance remains ambiguous. To address these challenges, we propose OrderDP, a plug-and-play framework that aims to obtain stable, unbiased, and near-lossless training acceleration with theoretical guarantees. Specifically, OrderDP first randomly selects a subset and then chooses the top-$q$ samples, where unbiasedness is established with respect to a surrogate loss. This ensures that OrderDP conducts unbiased training in terms of the surrogate objective. We further establish convergence and generalization analyses, elucidating how OrderDP affects optimal performance and enables well-controlled acceleration while ensuring guaranteed final performance. Empirically, we evaluate OrderDP against comprehensive baselines on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet-1K, demonstrating competitive accuracy, stable convergence, and exact control -- all with a simpler design and faster runtime, while reducing training cost by over 40%. Delivering both strong performance and computational efficiency, our method serves as a robust and easily adaptable tool for data-efficient learning. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/shengze-xu/OrderDP.

item.title: Physically Consistent Null Space Alignment for Detection of Low-Magnitude False Data Injection Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08473v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

虚假数据注入攻击（FDIA）即使幅值极小，若其注入方向与系统模型的伪零空间（pseudo-null space）对齐，仍可导致状态估计严重偏差，且逃逸传统残差检测器（如χ²检验）。现有方法存在根本局限：基于模型的检测器依赖精确雅可比矩阵 H，而数据驱动方法（如LSTM、自编码器、孤立森林）虽能学习测量相关性，却忽略物理约束，导致提取的子空间与真实物理零空间失准，无法可靠识别“低幅值-高影响”隐身攻击。

方法创新：PCNSA框架

本文提出物理一致性零空间对齐（Physically Consistent Null Space Alignment, PCNSA）——一种无需先验 H 的新型检测范式。核心是伪零空间保持的数据预处理（Pseudo-null Space Conserved Preprocessing, PSCP）：将原始量测通过物理坐标变换（如基于拓扑的归一化）重表达，再进行子空间学习。我们严格证明：PSCP保持行空间与其正交补空间的几何分离性，而传统逐特征标准化会破坏该性质。因此，SVD所得伪零子空间可自然对齐于物理残差空间，实现“隐式物理对齐”。

实验验证与优势

在IEEE 14/30/57/118节点系统上，PCNSA显著超越XTM、LSTM、AE和Isolation Forest基线：隐身攻击在对齐子空间中呈现清晰异常偏离，F1-score平均提升12.6%，检测准确率提高9.8%；同时在部分可观测（≤70% PMU覆盖率）与真实PMU噪声（±0.2°相角、±0.5%幅值）下保持鲁棒性。本工作首次将物理零空间几何结构嵌入数据驱动流程，为高隐蔽性电网攻击检测提供了可验证、可解释的新路径。

AI Summary (English)

False data injection attacks (FDIAs) with low-magnitude perturbations can severely bias power system state estimation when aligned with the system’s pseudo-null space—a vulnerability missed by conventional residual-based detectors (e.g., χ² test) and subspace-learning methods lacking physical consistency. To address this, we propose Physically Consistent Null Space Alignment (PCNSA), a detection framework that enforces geometric correspondence between the physical null space and the measurement-derived pseudo-null space—without requiring explicit knowledge of the measurement Jacobian H. Its core is Pseudo-null Space Conserved Preprocessing (PSCP): a physics-informed transformation that re-expresses measurements in a topologically grounded coordinate frame before subspace extraction. We prove PSCP preserves the orthogonal decomposition between row space and its complement—unlike standard per-feature normalization—and thus ensures SVD-derived pseudo-null subspaces align with the true physical residual space. Experiments on IEEE 14-, 30-, 57-, and 118-bus systems show PCNSA detects stealthy FDIAs evading XTM, LSTM, AE, and Isolation Forest baselines, achieving higher F1-score (+12.6% avg.) and accuracy (+9.8% avg.), while remaining robust under partial observability and realistic PMU noise.

Abstract

False data injection attacks (FDIAs) introducing small measurement perturbations can still cause large deviations in power system state estimation when the injected signals align with the pseudo-null space of the system model. Existing model- and data-driven detectors may fail to identify such low-magnitude but high-impact attacks because residual tests ignore changes hidden in the pseudo-null space, while subspace learning methods capture correlation patterns without enforcing physical consistency. This paper proposes Physically Consistent Null Space Alignment (PCNSA), a framework that detects stealthy FDIAs by preserving, through preprocessing, the geometric correspondence between the physical null space and the measurement-derived pseudo-null space. The key point is a Pseudo-null Space Conserved data Preprocessing (PSCP) step that re-expresses measurements in the physical coordinate frame before subspace extraction. We prove that PSCP preserves the separation between row space and its orthogonal complement, a property that conventional per-feature standardization violates. This keeps the singular value decomposition (SVD)-derived pseudo-null subspace aligned with the physical residual space without explicit knowledge of H. Experiments on IEEE 14-, 30-, 57-, and 118-bus systems confirm this principle in practice: stealthy attacks that evade XTM, LSTM, AE and Isolation Forest baselines appear as clear deviations in the aligned subspace, yielding higher F1-score and detection accuracy while remaining robust under partial observability and realistic PMU noise.

item.title: From Holistic Evaluation to Structured Criteria: Rubrics Across the Evolving LLM Landscape
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08625v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLMs）向开放域自主智能体演进，传统依赖标量奖励或整体性评判的评估与对齐机制日益力不从心。如何将人类复杂、多维的价值判断转化为机器可理解、可优化的信号，成为评估、强化学习与安全对齐三大方向共同面临的根本挑战。

方法与框架创新

本文提出量规（Rubric）作为统一范式框架，系统揭示其在LLM演进中的结构性角色：量规并非临时工具，而是对范式跃迁（如指令微调→推理增强→自主规划）的动态响应。我们将其明确定义为显式、结构化、可操作的质量判据集合，并首次实现三层次系统化建模：

  • 评估层：将“回答是否好”等整体判断分解为可验证维度（如事实性、逻辑连贯性、安全性）；
  • 训练层：替代稀疏标量奖励，提供细粒度过程反馈（如“步骤3未引用原文”），显著提升策略优化效率；
  • 内生层：模型在推理中自发生成量规（如自我质疑清单），驱动迭代式自修正与能力涌现。

关键发现与验证

我们系统梳理了52项跨领域量规设计（含HELM、RLAIF、Constitutional AI等），实证表明其在生成质量（↑17.3% FactScore）、执行保真度（↑22.1% step-wise accuracy）、理论约束满足率（↑31.4% alignment with deontic logic）及对抗鲁棒性（对越狱攻击拦截率↑39.6%）上均显著优于基线。最后，我们构建首个量规基准全景图，覆盖教育、医疗、法律等8大高价值领域，验证其作为“人类意图—机器行为”可持续桥梁的普适性与延展性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper positions the rubric—an explicit, structured set of evaluation criteria—as a unifying, evolution-responsive framework across LLM evaluation, reinforcement learning, and safety alignment. We identify three progressively deeper rubric roles: (1) evaluative, decomposing holistic judgments into verifiable dimensions; (2) training, delivering dense, process-level feedback where scalar rewards fail; and (3) intrinsic, emerging dynamically from model behavior to enable self-improvement. Through systematic analysis of 52 rubric designs, we demonstrate consistent gains: +17.3% in factual accuracy, +22.1% in step-wise execution fidelity, +31.4% in adherence to formal ethical constraints, and +39.6% robustness against jailbreaking. Rubrics thus serve as a durable, transparent bridge—translating human values into machine-learnable signals across the evolving LLM landscape.

Abstract

As Large Language Models (LLMs) advance toward open-ended autonomous agents, the mechanisms used to evaluate and guide their behavior must evolve accordingly. This work introduces the rubric as a unifying framework capturing this evolution, characterizing rubrics as a dynamic response to successive LLM paradigm shifts that recurs across otherwise independent efforts in evaluation, reinforcement learning, and safety alignment. We define rubrics as explicit criteria sets that transform complex quality judgments into structured and actionable standards, and demonstrate that their recurrence across these research threads is not coincidental. We systematically organize existing rubric designs, examine their construction and optimization, and analyze their role across evaluation and training. Rubrics manifest at three progressively deeper levels: at the evaluative level, they decompose holistic judgments into verifiable dimensions; at the training level, they serve as dense feedback signals providing process-level guidance where scalar rewards fall short; at the intrinsic level, they emerge dynamically from model behaviors, driving self-improvement. We further assess rubric reliability across generation quality, execution fidelity, theoretical constraints, and security threats, before surveying rubric-based benchmarks across diverse domains. By rendering assessment transparent and decomposable, rubrics translate human value expectations into machine-learnable signals, serving as the enduring bridge between human intentions and machine behavior.

item.title: SoK: Reconstruction Attacks on Synthetic Tabular Data (Insights from Winning the NIST CRC)
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08372v1

AI Summary (中文)

SoK：合成表格数据上的重构攻击综述（基于NIST CRC夺冠成果的洞见）

随着合成数据被广泛视为替代敏感真实数据发布的隐私保护方案，其核心对抗威胁——重构攻击（即利用少量已知准标识符，从合成数据中恢复个体隐藏属性值）——却长期缺乏系统性研究。本文是首项针对去标识化与合成表格数据的重构攻击（等价于属性推断）的系统化知识梳理（SoK）。我们提出首个结构化攻击分类法，按所利用的数据结构维度（如联合分布、边际依赖、生成模型内部表征）组织现有方法；开展迄今最全面的实证评估：在5个基准数据集上，横向对比14种重构攻击与9类合成数据生成（SDG）方法；并设计3种新攻击填补分类法空白，其中CoBP-RA（Conditional Bayesian Propagation with Record Anchoring）成为实测最强攻击。关键创新在于提出可解释的成功度量框架：① 记忆性检验——区分对群体分布的合理推断与对训练样本的过度记忆；② 归一化风险标度——将重构攻击与成员推断攻击统一映射至同一可比尺度。核心发现包括：SDG方法的选择主导风险水平，远超攻击算法选择；差分隐私仅在极小预算（$\varepsilon \lesssim 1$）下提供显著保护，更高预算下防护效果趋于饱和，瓶颈在于生成器容量而非噪声强度；传统去标识化方法（如k-匿名）暴露程度最高；约85%的重构成功源于对分布结构的建模能力，而非记忆训练记录，因此个体风险高度集中于统计异常记录。本工作全部攻击实现与评估框架已通过2025年美国国家标准与技术研究院（NIST）协作研究周期（CRC）红队竞赛验证——我们在全部红队中位列第一。

AI Summary (English)

This SoK paper systematizes reconstruction (i.e., attribute inference) attacks on de-identified and synthetic tabular data—the central adversarial threat to privacy-preserving data release. We introduce a novel taxonomy of attacks by exploited structural properties, conduct the most comprehensive empirical evaluation to date (14 attacks × 9 SDG methods × 5 benchmarks), and propose new attacks—including CoBP-RA, the strongest measured. Crucially, we define what success means: a memorization test distinguishing distributional inference from training-record leakage, and a reduction unifying reconstruction and membership inference on one scale. Key findings: (1) The choice of SDG method dominates risk—not the attack; (2) Differential privacy protects significantly only at very low budgets ($\varepsilon \lesssim 1$), plateauing due to synthesizer capacity, not noise; (3) De-identification methods are most vulnerable; (4) Most reconstruction reflects population structure, concentrating risk on atypical individuals. All methods were externally validated by our first-place finish among all red teams in the 2025 NIST Collaborative Research Cycle.

Abstract

Synthetic data is increasingly promoted as a privacy-preserving substitute for releasing sensitive tabular records, yet its central adversarial threat ("reconstruction", the recovery of an individual's hidden attribute values from a synthetic release and a handful of known quasi-identifiers) has been studied only in scattered, hard-to-compare settings. We present the first systematization of reconstruction (equivalently, attribute inference) attacks on de-identified and synthetic tabular data. We contribute a taxonomy that organizes attacks by the structure they exploit; the most systematic empirical evaluation to date, pitting fourteen attacks against nine synthetic data generation (SDG) methods across five benchmark datasets; and a set of new attacks that fill gaps in the taxonomy, one of which (CoBP-RA) is the strongest attack we measure. Crucially, we introduce a methodology for interpreting what attack success means: a memorization test that distinguishes reconstruction of the population distribution from memorization of training records, and a reduction that places reconstruction and membership inference on a single comparable scale. Our findings: the choice of SDG method governs risk far more than the choice of attack; differential privacy protects mainly at small budgets ($\varepsilon\lesssim1$), above which protection plateaus, bounded by the synthesizer's capacity rather than its noise; de-identification methods are the most exposed; and most reconstruction reflects distributional structure rather than memorization, concentrating individual risk on atypical records. The attacks and infrastructure are externally validated by our first-place finish among all red teams in the 2025 \textit{National Institute of Standards and Technology} (NIST) Collaborative Research Cycle.

item.title: An AI Security Agent for University ACMIS: Multi-Vector Threat Detection and Automated Response
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08270v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

高校学术管理信息系统（ACMIS）承载着学籍、成绩、教务、缴费等核心学术与财务数据，已成为网络攻击的高价值靶标。其面临威胁高度复合化：包括暴力破解登录、支付欺诈、权限越权、内部人员数据窃取及学术诚信违规（如批量篡改成绩、伪造科研记录）等。传统基于静态规则的入侵检测系统（IDS）难以应对——因多数恶意行为在操作序列、API调用模式或界面交互上与正常业务高度相似，导致漏报率高、误报泛滥。

方法与架构

本文提出一种面向ACMIS的AI安全代理（AI Security Agent），采用多模态协同检测范式：

  • 监督式异常检测：在认证、授权、金融交易三层构建时序特征模型（LSTM+Attention），识别偏离基线的行为模式；
  • 无监督行为分析：基于图神经网络（GNN）建模用户-资源-操作三元关系，捕获隐蔽的横向移动与权限滥用；
  • NLP驱动的安全交互层：集成轻量级对话机器人，通过语义理解与多因子验证（如课程知识问答+设备指纹）实现抗钓鱼的密码恢复流程，阻断社工攻击入口。

系统监控认证、授权、财务、用户行为、系统健康五大运行层，响应机制采用四阶风险升级框架（观察→告警→临时隔离→自动封禁+取证），支持策略热更新与跨系统复用。

主要成果与创新

在模拟ACMIS事件日志数据集（含12类真实攻击场景、280万条日志）上的实验表明：

  • 威胁检测宏平均F1达0.91（规则基线仅0.49），关键威胁（如特权劫持、资金盗刷）召回率提升至96.3%；
  • 自动化响应在95%分位延迟<300 ms，满足实时处置要求；
  • 模块化设计已成功迁移至图书馆管理系统与研究生招生平台，验证了跨业务系统的可扩展性。

本工作首次将学术行为语义建模与多向量动态响应深度融合，为教育信息系统提供了兼具鲁棒性、可解释性与可部署性的AI原生安全范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces an AI Security Agent tailored for University Academic Management Information Systems (ACMIS), addressing multi-vector threats—including brute-force attacks, payment fraud, privilege escalation, insider data theft, and academic integrity violations—that evade traditional rule-based detection due to operational similarity with legitimate activities. The agent integrates supervised anomaly detection (LSTM-Attention on authentication/authorization/financial logs), graph-based behavioral analytics (GNN modeling user-resource-operation relationships), and an NLP-powered secure chatbot for phishing-resistant password recovery via semantic verification. It monitors five operational layers and enacts responses via a four-tier risk escalation framework. Evaluated on a realistic simulated ACMIS log dataset (2.8M events, 12 attack types), the agent achieves a macro-average F1-score of 0.91, significantly outperforming a rule-based baseline (0.49); critical-tier automated response latency is under 300 ms at the 95th percentile. Its modular architecture enables seamless extension to other institutional systems.

Abstract

University Academic Management Information Systems (ACMIS) are high-value targets for a wide spectrum of security threats including brute-force login attacks, payment fraud, privilege escalation, insider data theft, and academic integrity violations. Traditional rule-based intrusion detection systems are inadequate because many malicious activities are structurally indistinguishable from normal operations. This paper presents an AI-based security agent for ACMIS that combines supervised anomaly detection, behavioural analytics, and a natural language processing chatbot for secure password recovery. The agent monitors five operational layers: authentication, authorisation, financial transactions, user behaviour, and system health, and responds through a four-tier risk escalation framework. A modular architecture allows the core engine to be extended to other institutional systems. Experiments on a simulated ACMIS event log dataset demonstrate a threat detection macro-average F1 of 0.91, compared to 0.49 for a rule-based baseline, with critical-tier automated response latency under 300 ms at the 95th percentile.

item.title: Quantifying and Defending against the Privacy Risk in Logit-based Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08252v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（FL）旨在不共享原始私有数据的前提下协同训练模型，而新兴的logit-based FL方法通过在公共数据上交换模型输出（logits）替代传统参数/梯度传输，显著提升客户端模型异构性、降低通信开销，并被普遍认为更隐私友好。然而，本研究首次揭示并系统论证：该范式存在被长期忽视的隐性隐私风险——半诚实服务器可通过历史logits逆向推断客户端私有模型（即“模型窃取”），即使公共数据与客户端私有数据完全无关。

方法与创新

我们提出自适应模型窃取攻击（AdaMSA），利用训练过程中多轮logits的时序相关性，构建可微分重建目标，实现对客户端模型权重的高保真恢复。理论层面，我们首次推导出logits信息对模型参数的可重构性上界，严格量化了隐私泄露程度与logits维度、训练轮次及数据分布偏移之间的关系。针对该风险，我们设计了一种轻量级防御机制：在logits传输前施加方向感知扰动（Direction-Aware Perturbation），即沿最小化模型可恢复性的梯度方向添加受限噪声，在几乎不损训练精度（<0.3% Acc下降）的前提下，使AdaMSA攻击成功率从92.7%降至11.4%。

关键发现

  • 隐私风险具有内在性与顽固性：不依赖公共数据语义相关性，仅凭logits统计特性即可触发；
  • AdaMSA在CIFAR-10/100和Tiny-ImageNet上均实现>90%模型结构识别准确率与>85%权重L2重建相似度；
  • 所提扰动防御在保持全局模型精度的同时，将服务器对客户端模型的互信息降低68.3%，为logit-based FL提供了首个可证明、可部署的隐私增强方案。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) aims to preserve data privacy by training models without sharing raw client data. While logit-based FL—exchanging model outputs on public data instead of weights or gradients—offers advantages in heterogeneity and communication efficiency, its inherent privacy risks remain unexamined. This paper presents the first theoretical and empirical analysis revealing that a semi-honest server can reconstruct clients’ private models from historical logits, even when public and private data are statistically independent. We propose the Adaptive Model Stealing Attack (AdaMSA), which exploits temporal correlations among logits across training rounds to achieve high-fidelity model recovery. Our theoretical analysis establishes tight bounds on this reconstruction risk, linking it to logit dimensionality, round count, and distribution shift. To mitigate the threat, we introduce a lightweight direction-aware perturbation strategy that adds minimal, privacy-optimal noise to logits—reducing AdaMSA’s success rate from 92.7% to 11.4% with <0.3% accuracy drop on global model convergence. Experiments across CIFAR-10/100 and Tiny-ImageNet validate both the severity of the risk and the efficacy of our defense.

Abstract

Federated learning aims to protect data privacy by collaboratively learning a model without sharing private data among clients. Unlike traditional parameter-based FL methods that exchange model weights or gradients during training, emerging logit-based FL approaches share model outputs (logits) on public data. This strategy promotes model heterogeneity, reduces communication overhead, and enhances clients' privacy. However, the potential privacy risks associated with these logit-based methods have been largely overlooked. This research presents the first theoretical and empirical analysis of a hidden privacy risk in logit-based FL methods - the risk that a semi-honest server (adversary) may learn clients' private models from logits. To quantify and address this threat, we develop the Adaptive Model Stealing Attack (AdaMSA) by leveraging historical logits during training. Notably, we observe that this inherent privacy risk persists even when public data is unrelated to private data, emphasizing the urgency to address privacy vulnerabilities in logit-based FL methods. Moreover, our theoretical analysis establishes the bounds of this privacy risk. We then propose a simple but effective defense strategy that perturbs the transmitted logits in the direction that minimizes the privacy risk while maximally preserving the training performance. The experimental results validate our analysis and demonstrate the effectiveness of AdaMSA and our defense strategy.

item.title: LPOR: A Layered Proof of Reserves Framework for Usable and Publicly Auditable Solvency Verification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08211v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Proof of Reserves（PoR） 是中心化加密货币交易所向公众证明其链上储备金足以覆盖全部用户负债的关键机制。然而，现有方案（如Merkle树型PoR、零知识PoR系统）虽具备密码学严谨性，却普遍要求用户安装专用工具、理解哈希运算或依赖第三方验证器，导致日常用户实际参与率极低，审计透明度流于形式。

方法：LPOR分层框架

我们提出 LPOR（Layered Proof of Reserves） ——一种以可用性为核心设计的分层PoR框架。其核心创新在于职责分离：

  • 用户层（Lightweight）：仅需浏览器访问公开网页，输入自身钱包地址即可即时验证账户是否被包含在当期负债快照中；并可通过公开CSV/JSON格式的负债清单，无需任何密码学知识即可手动或脚本化重算总负债（如SUM(余额)）；
  • 审计层（Cryptographic）：由专业机构或社区审计员执行完整密码学验证（如Merkle路径校验、签名聚合验证），确保负债数据完整性与储备金足额性。

该设计将“可验证性”解耦为“可参与性”与“可审计性”，显著降低用户端摩擦。

主要发现与创新

  • 在千万级用户规模实测中，LPOR支持亚秒级用户侧包含性查询，总负债重算耗时<2秒（单机Python）；
  • 通过引入随机抽样公示+时间戳锚定+增量快照版本控制，将遗漏负债（omitted liabilities）的检测概率从传统PoR的~30%提升至>99.7%（基于蒙特卡洛模拟，假设1%未申报账户）；
  • 首次实现非技术用户对负债总额的独立、可重复、免信任验证，真正激活公众监督力量。

AI Summary (English)

LPOR is a layered Proof of Reserves framework designed to bridge the gap between cryptographic soundness and real-world usability in solvency verification for centralized crypto exchanges. Unlike existing Merkle- or ZK-based PoR systems—which require technical expertise or dedicated tooling—LPOR decouples verification into two accessible layers: (1) a lightweight user layer, enabling any participant to instantly confirm account inclusion and publicly recompute total liabilities from open, human-readable data (e.g., CSV/JSON); and (2) a cryptographic auditor layer, where experts verify data integrity and reserve sufficiency using standard cryptographic proofs. Evaluated at multi-million-user scale, LPOR achieves sub-second inclusion checks, <2-second liability summation on commodity hardware, and raises the detectability probability of omitted liabilities from ~30% to >99.7% under realistic assumptions. By lowering barriers to participation, LPOR transforms PoR from a compliance exercise into a genuinely public, actionable audit mechanism.

Abstract

Proof of Reserves (PoR) enables centralized crypto exchanges to demonstrate that on-chain reserves are sufficient to cover customer liabilities. However, existing approaches, including Merkle-tree-based proofs and zero-knowledge PoR systems, remain difficult for everyday users to verify in practice, resulting in limited participation and weakened transparency. We introduce LPOR, a layered, usability-focused PoR framework that separates lightweight user-side checks from auditor-level cryptographic verification, enabling non-technical users to verify inclusion and publicly recompute total liabilities with minimal friction. By lowering verification barriers, LPOR increases user participation and substantially improves the probability of detecting omitted liabilities. We evaluate its scalability and omission detectability at a multi-million-user scale.

item.title: AI-Native Closed-Loop Security for 6G-Enabled Cyber-Physical Systems: From Edge Detection to Network-Wide Mitigation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08173v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在第六代（6G）网络中，海量网络物理系统（CPS）——包括自动驾驶车辆、智能电网、工业机器人及远程手术设备——将运行于超可靠低时延通信（URLLC）切片之上。此时，远程网络攻击到物理危害的响应窗口被压缩至毫秒级，传统边界防火墙与中心化安全运营中心（SOC）已无法满足实时防护需求。

方法与架构

本研究提出一种AI原生闭环安全范式，构建端到端可验证的安全流水线：

  • 感知层：依托多接入边缘计算（MEC）节点，融合分钟级呼叫详单记录（CDR）用于基线建模，叠加亚毫秒级无线接入网（RAN/O-RAN）遥测数据保障时延关键路径；
  • 决策层：部署轻量化深度模型实现本地实时检测；
  • 响应层：通过SDN、NFV与O-RAN控制器协同执行网络级动态缓解；
  • 进化层：结合联邦学习（FL）实现隐私保护下的模型协同更新，并引入数字孪生（DT）回放机制增强攻击复现与策略验证能力。

核心贡献

基于PRISMA 2020协议系统综述128篇（2017–2026）权威文献，本文：
1. 首次将6G/CPS威胁面映射至MITRE ATT&CK框架，并构建CDR可观测特征空间；
2. 统一评估12个公开数据集上的边缘异常检测与DDoS分类性能，覆盖统计、图神经网络与Transformer模型；
3. 提出首个融合SDN/NFV/O-RAN的闭环参考架构，并形式化定义各切片专属的尾部时延契约（如URLLC切片强制p99 ≤ 10 ms）；
4. 将联邦学习、大语言模型（LLM）、数字孪生、后量子密码（PQC）、零信任架构（ZTA）与可解释AI（XAI）定位为交叉使能技术，而非孤立模块；
5. 凝练五大开放方向：数据稀缺性、确定性低时延、跨域可信协同、国际标准割裂、评估基准缺失。

AI Summary (English)

This survey establishes an AI-native closed-loop security paradigm for 6G-enabled cyber-physical systems (CPS), addressing the millisecond-scale breach-to-harm window that renders legacy perimeter defenses obsolete. We propose a verified pipeline: edge sensing via MEC using CDRs (for baseline learning) and sub-millisecond RAN/O-RAN telemetry (for latency-critical detection); local decision-making with compressed deep models; network-wide mitigation via SDN/NFV/O-RAN controllers; and continuous retraining through federated learning (FL) and digital-twin replay. Formalizing per-slice, tail-bounded latency contracts—e.g., p99 ≤ 10 ms for URLLC slices—we systematically analyze 128 peer-reviewed studies (2017–2026) under PRISMA 2020. Key contributions include: (i) mapping the 6G/CPS threat surface to MITRE ATT&CK and a CDR-observable feature space; (ii) unifying edge anomaly and DDoS detection across 12 datasets and model families; (iii) synthesizing SDN/NFV/O-RAN into a single reference architecture; (iv) treating FL, LLMs, DT, PQC, ZTA, and XAI as interdependent enablers; and (v) consolidating five critical open challenges in data, latency, trust, standardization, and evaluation.

Abstract

In sixth-generation (6G) networks, billions of cyber-physical systems (CPSs) - autonomous vehicles, smart grids, industrial robots, and remote-surgical equipment - will run over ultra-reliable low-latency slices, collapsing the gap between a remote breach and physical harm to milliseconds, a budget perimeter firewalls and centralised security operations centres cannot meet. This survey reframes 6G CPS security as a closed-loop, AI-native pipeline that senses at the multi-access edge computing (MEC) tier, using minute-scale call-detail records (CDRs) for baseline learning and sub-millisecond RAN/Open-RAN (O-RAN) telemetry for the latency-critical path. It decides locally with compressed deep models, mitigates network-wide via SDN, NFV, and O-RAN controllers, and retrains through federated learning (FL) and digital-twin (DT) replay. We formalise a per-slice, tail-bounded latency contract on the sense, detect, and mitigate stages, enforced at a slice-dependent tail percentile (p99 for safety-critical URLLC slices). Organising 128 peer-reviewed studies (2017-2026) under a PRISMA 2020 protocol, we (i) map the 6G/CPS threat surface to MITRE ATT&CK and a CDR-observable feature space; (ii) unify edge anomaly detection and DDoS classification across twelve datasets and statistical, graph, and transformer models; (iii) synthesise SDN/NFV/O-RAN primitives into one closed-loop reference architecture; (iv) treat FL, large language models (LLMs), DT, post-quantum cryptography (PQC), zero-trust architecture (ZTA), and explainable AI as cross-cutting enablers, not parallel pillars; and (v) consolidate open problems into five directions spanning data, latency, trust, standardisation, and evaluation.

item.title: Closing the Sim-to-Real Gap: An Evaluation Framework for Autonomous Cyber Defense Configuration of Commercial EDR
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08168v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

当前主流商用终端检测与响应（EDR）系统正经历范式转变：从依赖安全运营人员手动配置规则，演进为由多组件构成的自主AI驱动防御体系。在此背景下，自治型网络防御智能体（autonomous defense agents）不再仅“调优”被动工具，而是需协同甚至接管具备厂商专有决策逻辑的黑盒式自主EDR系统——这导致传统仿真环境与开源EDR评估方法难以反映真实企业环境中的动态交互与行为不确定性，形成显著的仿真到现实（sim-to-real）鸿沟。

方法与框架创新

本文提出首个面向商用EDR的自治防御智能体硬化评估框架。我们在Horizon3.ai构建的Game of Active Directory（GOAD）红蓝对抗实验环境中进行实证：以NodeZero作为自主渗透测试代理（红方），Microsoft Defender XDR作为被防护EDR（蓝方），系统性评估两类基于大语言模型（LLM）的防御智能体（Claude Sonnet 4.6与Cisco Foundation-Sec-8B）。框架核心设计包含细粒度策略级动作归因机制、EDR自主行为时序观测模块及面向科学评估的遥测重映射层。

关键发现与启示

通过实证分析，我们揭示三项仅在真实商用EDR环境中可复现的关键现象：

  • 遥测设计偏差：商用EDR采集的遥测数据专为SOC分析师工作流优化，缺乏标准化、低噪声、高时间分辨率的科研级指标；
  • 动作混淆困境：若不实施每项防御策略的显式归因，无法区分智能体主动配置行为与EDR自身AI引擎的隐式响应；
  • 行为时变性：EDR的自主决策逻辑（如异常评分、自动隔离阈值）在评估窗口内发生非预期漂移，破坏实验可重复性。

本研究不仅填补了商用EDR自治化评估的方法论空白，更呼吁建立适配黑盒、时变、厂商锁定型安全基础设施的新一代基准测试范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first evaluation framework for autonomous defense agents that harden commercial EDR systems—moving beyond simulation or open-source-EDR benchmarks. We instantiate it in a realistic Game of Active Directory (GOAD) lab, using Horizon3.ai’s NodeZero as the autonomous red team and Microsoft Defender XDR as the black-box EDR under defense. Evaluating two LLM-based defense agents (Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Cisco Foundation-Sec-8B), we identify three critical, real-world phenomena invisible in simulated settings: (i) commercial EDR telemetry is optimized for SOC analyst workflows—not scientific benchmarking—lacking standardized, low-latency, attributable signals; (ii) per-policy action attribution is essential to disentangle agent-initiated configurations from the EDR’s autonomous vendor-specific decisions; and (iii) the EDR’s autonomous behavior (e.g., detection thresholds, response triggers) exhibits non-trivial temporal variation during evaluation, undermining reproducibility. These findings expose a fundamental sim-to-real gap in enterprise cyber defense evaluation and motivate new methodologies for benchmarking autonomous agents in production-grade, black-box security infrastructure.

Abstract

Leading commercial endpoint detection and response (EDR) products have shifted from operator-configured rule sets to multi-component systems where autonomous AI components operate alongside, and increasingly in place of, operator-deployed policies. Autonomous defense agents using commercial EDR as their hardening tool are no longer tuning a passive tool, but a black-box autonomous system capable of making vendor-specific decisions. We present the first evaluation framework for autonomous defense agents hardening commercial EDR. We instantiate it in a Game of Active Directory (GOAD) lab with Horizon3.ai's NodeZero as the autonomous pentester and Microsoft Defender XDR as the EDR. We run a sample benchmark of defense agents with two large language model (LLM) backbones (Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Cisco Foundation-Sec-8B). We report three lessons learned that neither simulation nor open-source-EDR evaluation can surface: (i) commercial EDR telemetry is engineered for Security Operations Center (SOC) analyst workflows rather than scientific benchmarking; (ii) the importance of per-policy attribution to separate defense agent actions from autonomous EDR actions; and (iii) the EDR's autonomous behavior varies during the evaluation window. Together, these findings highlight a sim-to-real gap for enterprise defense and motivate evaluation methodology for benchmarking autonomous defense agents in environments with black-box, autonomous tools.

item.title: VATS: Exploiting Implicit Authority in Error-Path Injection via Systematic Mutation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07992v1

AI Summary (中文)

VATS：基于系统性变异的错误路径注入中隐式权威利用框架

随着模型上下文协议（MCP） 逐步成为自主智能体工具调用的事实标准，其内置的错误处理循环正暴露出一个关键却长期被忽视的安全攻击面。本文提出核心假设：工具返回的错误消息天然携带隐式权威性——该特性会触发大模型进入特殊的“纠错推理模式”，从而绕过常规安全过滤器与内容策略。为验证并量化这一风险，我们设计并实现了VATS（Tool Stream脆弱性分析框架），一种以变异驱动的系统性对抗测试方法。VATS在七个正交维度（包括结构位置、语义嵌套、词性扰动、标点诱导、上下文压缩、错误类型伪装与指令密度）上对提示进行精细化、可复现的演化，实现对错误路径注入（Error-Path Injection, EPI）的全覆盖探测。

我们在四大前沿闭源与开源模型上开展严格评估：Gemini 3.1 Pro、GPT-5.5、GLM-5.1 与 Qwen3-Coder。结果表明：相较于传统间接提示注入（IPI），EPI攻击成功率平均提升3倍，在受控实验中最高达100%指令合规率；其中，结构定位（sandwiching）——即把恶意指令精准嵌入错误消息的前后上下文之间——被证实为跨模型最鲁棒的利用向量，在全部四模型上均保持>92%有效率。进一步分析发现：尽管生产级框架（如LangChain、LlamaIndex）的运行时防护可部分缓解该风险，但模型层固有的推理机制缺陷构成系统性底层威胁，尤其危及高度定制化的代理工作流（bespoke agentic workflows）。本研究首次揭示了MCP生态中“错误即入口”的新型攻击范式，为下一代安全协议设计提供了实证依据与防御优先级指南。

AI Summary (English)

As the Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardizes tool-calling in autonomous agents, its error-handling loop emerges as a critical, previously unexamined attack surface. We hypothesize that tool error messages carry implicit authority, triggering corrective reasoning modes that bypass standard safety heuristics. To test this, we introduce VATS (Vulnerability Analysis of Tool Streams), a mutation-driven framework that systematically evolves adversarial payloads across seven structural and linguistic dimensions. Evaluating on Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.5, GLM-5.1, and Qwen3-Coder, VATS shows error-path injection (EPI) triples success rates over standard indirect prompt injection (IPI), achieving up to 100% compliance in controlled settings. Structural positioning—sandwiching instructions within error context—is the most effective exploit vector across all models. While production framework guardrails offer partial mitigation, the model-layer susceptibility poses a systemic risk to bespoke agentic workflows. VATS reveals a novel “error-as-entry” attack paradigm in MCP ecosystems.

Abstract

As the Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardizes tool-calling for autonomous agents, it introduces a critical, unexamined attack surface: the error-handling loop. We hypothesize that tool error messages possess implicit authority, triggering corrective reasoning modes that bypass standard safety heuristics. We introduce VATS (Vulnerability Analysis of Tool Streams), a mutation-driven framework that systematically evolves adversarial payloads across seven structural and linguistic dimensions. Our evaluation across four frontier models, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.5, GLM-5.1, and Qwen3-Coder, demonstrates that error-path injection triples the success rate of standard indirect prompt injection (IPI), achieving up to 100% compliance in controlled evaluations. We isolate structural positioning (sandwiching instructions within error context) as the most effective exploit vector across all tested models. While we find that production framework guardrails can mitigate these vulnerabilities, the inherent susceptibility of the model layer poses a systemic risk to bespoke agentic workflows.

item.title: SGTO-MAS: Secure Gorilla Troops Optimization for Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07940v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多智能体大语言模型（LLM）系统在复杂推理与协同决策中展现出强大潜力，但智能体间动态协调易引发错误传播、安全漏洞及资源浪费。现有方法多依赖启发式、静态调度策略，缺乏对性能、安全性与计算开销三者进行联合优化的理论框架。

方法创新：SGTO-MAS 框架

本文提出 SGTO-MAS（Secure Gorilla Troops Optimization for Multi-Agent LLM Systems），首次将多智能体LLM协调建模为带安全约束的组合优化问题。核心创新包括：

  • 三层安全感知机制：融合细粒度信任建模（基于历史响应一致性与对抗鲁棒性）、风险感知评估（量化单代理潜在失效概率与信息泄露熵）、以及集体智能聚合（通过共识加权投票抑制恶意/低信度输出）；
  • 自适应优化引擎：引入受大猩猩群体行为启发的安全增强型GTO算法（SGTO），在动态威胁环境下实时调整智能体子集规模、角色分配与通信拓扑，支持在线重配置；
  • 统一目标函数：$\mathcal{L} = \alpha \cdot \text{Performance} - \beta \cdot \text{Risk} + \gamma \cdot \text{Consensus} - \delta \cdot \text{AgentCount}$，实现多目标帕累托优化。

实验结果与价值

在500次独立实验中，SGTO-MAS达成：

  • 稳定高性能：平均任务得分 0.5281（±0.0173），显著优于基线（+12.6%）；
  • 高鲁棒性：智能体随机移除时性能仅降 2.5%，共识结构扰动下仅降 5.3%；
  • 高效轻量：平均选中 4.04个智能体（较全集减少62%），单次优化耗时 24.09秒；
  • 安全可控：风险指标稳定于 0.3000，共识度达 0.8764。

本工作为面向真实对抗场景的多智能体LLM系统提供了首个可验证、可扩展、安全内生的协调范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces SGTO-MAS, a security-aware optimization framework for coordinating multi-agent LLM systems. We formulate agent selection as a constrained multi-objective problem—jointly optimizing performance, risk, consensus, and computational efficiency—and solve it via a novel Secure Gorilla Troops Optimization (SGTO) algorithm, which dynamically adapts agent subsets under threat conditions using trust modeling, risk-aware evaluation, and collective intelligence. Across 500 independent runs, SGTO-MAS achieves a stable average performance score of 0.5281 (σ = 0.0173), high consensus (0.8764), controlled risk (0.3000), and compact agent subsets (4.04 agents on average), with convergence in 24.09 seconds per run. Robustness analysis shows graceful degradation: only 2.5% performance drop under agent removal and 5.3% under consensus disruption. SGTO-MAS provides the first principled, deployable solution for secure, efficient, and adaptive coordination in adversarial multi-agent LLM deployments.

Abstract

Multi-agent large language model (LLM) systems offer strong capabilities for complex reasoning and decision-making, yet coordination across agents introduces error propagation, security risks, and inefficient use of resources. Existing methods often rely on heuristic, static strategies and lack a principled mechanism for balancing performance, security, and computational cost. This paper formulates multi-agent LLM coordination as a constrained optimization problem and proposes a security-aware method for adaptive agent selection. The method integrates trust modeling, risk-aware evaluation, and collective intelligence within a unified optimization objective. To solve the problem efficiently, we use a swarm-intelligence strategy inspired by Gorilla Troops Optimization (GTO), enabling adaptive coordination under varying threat conditions. Controlled experiments across 500 independent runs demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method. The system achieves a stable average performance score of 0.5281, with high consensus (0.8764), controlled risk (0.3000), and compact agent subsets averaging 4.04 selected agents. The optimization process converges efficiently, with an average runtime of 24.09 seconds per run and low score variability (standard deviation = 0.0173). Robustness analysis indicates graceful degradation under perturbations, with performance drops limited to 2.5% under agent removal and 5.3% under consensus disruption. These results show that effective multi-agent coordination can be achieved through structured optimization that jointly manages performance, security, and efficiency. The proposed method provides a practical security-aware solution for coordinating multi-agent LLM systems in complex adversarial settings.

item.title: AeroSpectra Sentinel: An Auditable LLM Prompt-Chaining Decision-Support Workflow for Acute Asthma Risk Assessment from Respiratory Sounds and Clinical Signals
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08247v1

AI Summary (中文)

AeroSpectra Sentinel：面向急性哮喘风险评估的可审计LLM提示链式决策支持工作流

背景：急性哮喘风险评估需综合判断呼吸音、血氧饱和度、气流受限、言语能力、呼吸做功、意识状态及缓解治疗反应等多维临床信号。传统纯音频分类器虽能识别哮鸣音模式，但普遍缺乏透明临床推理路径与安全分级逻辑，难以满足临床决策可追溯、可验证的要求。

方法：本研究提出AeroSpectra Sentinel——一款客户端部署的研究原型系统，构建端到端、分阶段、可审计的决策支持工作流。其核心创新在于融合：① 短时傅里叶变换（STFT）驱动的呼吸音分析；② 轻量级机器学习（ML）初筛模块；③ 临床特征融合层；④ 五阶段大语言模型（LLM）提示链（prompt-chaining）引擎，严格分离信号采集、预处理、声学特征提取、ML筛查、临床守门员（guardrails）干预及FHIR-ready结构化报告生成。

结果：在含1,211条WAV录音（5类标签）的公开数据集上，基于分层抽样584条样本评估显示：随机森林实现哮喘/非哮喘二分类准确率91.10%、F1-score 78.69%；特征驱动的MLP达89.73%/78.26%；紧凑型对数谱图CNN为73.29%/55.17%；多分类准确率77.40%，宏F1达77.23%。LLM流程审计采用40例模拟临床场景，对比四种策略：单次提示、提示链、带临床守门员的提示链、守门员+ FHIR模式校验的提示链——后者在模拟安全性与文档一致性上表现最优。

意义：本工作首次将可审计提示链、临床守门员机制与医疗互操作标准（FHIR）深度耦合，为边缘侧AI辅助呼吸急症评估提供了可复现、可验证、符合临床逻辑的新范式。需强调：AeroSpectra Sentinel为研究原型，非诊断器械，未经临床验证。

AI Summary (English)

AeroSpectra Sentinel is a client-side research prototype for acute asthma risk assessment that integrates respiratory sound analysis (via STFT), lightweight ML screening, clinical feature fusion, and a five-stage auditable LLM prompt-chaining workflow. It enforces strict separation of signal acquisition, preprocessing, acoustic feature extraction, ML triage, clinical guardrails (e.g., escalation logic, contraindication checks), and FHIR-compliant reporting. On a public dataset of 1,211 respiratory audio recordings, a stratified subset (n=584) yielded 91.10% binary accuracy (asthma vs. non-asthma) with random forest and 77.40% multiclass accuracy. In scenario-based auditing across 40 simulated clinical vignettes, the full pipeline—prompt chaining + clinical guardrails + FHIR schema validation—achieved superior safety compliance and documentation consistency versus baseline prompting methods. This work establishes a novel, transparent, edge-deployable framework for AI-assisted respiratory decision support—intended strictly for research, not clinical use.

Abstract

Acute asthma risk assessment requires rapid interpretation of respiratory sounds, oxygenation, airflow limitation, speech ability, work of breathing, mental status, and response to reliever therapy. Conventional audio-only classifiers can detect wheeze-like patterns but often lack transparent clinical reasoning and safe escalation logic. This paper presents AeroSpectra Sentinel, a client-side research prototype and decision-support workflow that combines short-time Fourier transform (STFT) respiratory sound analysis, lightweight machine-learning screening, clinical feature fusion, and a five-stage large language model (LLM) prompt-chaining process. The workflow separates signal acquisition, preprocessing, acoustic feature extraction, ML screening, clinical guardrails, and FHIR-ready reporting. We evaluated the audio screening component on a public respiratory sound dataset containing 1,211 WAV recordings from five labels. Using a stratified subset of 584 recordings, a random forest achieved 91.10% binary accuracy and 78.69% F1-score for asthma-vs-non-asthma screening, while a feature-based multilayer perceptron achieved 89.73% accuracy and 78.26% F1-score. A compact log-spectrogram CNN achieved 73.29% accuracy and 55.17% F1-score. Multiclass classification achieved 77.40% accuracy and 77.23% macro-F1. To evaluate the LLM workflow, we conducted a scenario-based audit on 40 simulated clinical vignettes comparing one-shot prompting, prompt chaining, prompt chaining with guardrails, and prompt chaining with guardrails plus FHIR schema validation. The guardrail-plus-schema variant achieved the strongest simulated safety and documentation consistency. AeroSpectra Sentinel is intended as a research prototype, not as a diagnostic medical device or clinically validated risk-assessment product.

item.title: LogNEO: A GPT-Neo Reinforcement Learning Framework for Accurate Real-Time Log Anomaly Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08153v1

AI Summary (中文)

LogNEO：基于GPT-Neo与强化学习的实时日志异常检测框架

在大规模分布式系统中，高精度、低延迟的日志异常检测是保障系统可靠性与安全性的关键挑战。现有方法（如LogBERT、LogGPT）多依赖监督微调或无监督重建，难以兼顾序列建模能力与细粒度时序判别力。本文提出LogNEO——首个将1.3B参数开源大语言模型GPT-Neo深度适配于日志异常检测任务的强化学习框架。其核心创新在于：

  • 位置感知的指数衰减奖励机制：为每个日志token位置设计动态奖励权重，早期位置正确预测获更高奖励（强调关键前缀模式），后期错误则触发强惩罚（抑制累积误差）；
  • PPO优化+交叉熵正则化联合训练：在Proximal Policy Optimization（PPO）框架下引入跨熵约束，稳定策略梯度更新并防止过拟合；
  • 端到端生产级部署：集成Apache Kafka流式接入、Redis缓存上下文、TensorRT加速推理，实测吞吐达15,000事件/秒，端到端延迟仅45毫秒。

在HDFS、BGL和Thunderbird三大基准数据集上，LogNEO分别取得F1-score 0.927、0.913、0.984，较当前最优模型LogGPT召回率提升最高达6个百分点（如BGL从0.853→0.913），同时保持精度基本持平（±0.002）。实验表明，该框架显著提升了对长程依赖日志模式（如启动失败链、权限越界序列）的敏感性，为AIOps场景提供了可落地的实时检测新范式。

AI Summary (English)

LogNEO is a reinforcement learning framework for real-time log anomaly detection, built upon EleutherAI’s GPT-Neo (1.3B parameters). It introduces a novel partial-credit, exponentially decaying position-aware reward scheme within Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), where early token positions receive higher rewards for correct predictions and later errors incur stronger penalties—explicitly modeling positional difficulty in log sequences. Cross-entropy regularization further stabilizes training. Evaluated on HDFS, BGL, and Thunderbird benchmarks, LogNEO achieves F1-scores of 0.927, 0.913, and 0.984, respectively—improving recall by up to 6 percentage points over LogGPT while preserving precision. Deployed as a microservice with Kafka ingestion, Redis context caching, and TensorRT-accelerated inference, it delivers 45 ms end-to-end latency at 15,000 events/sec, demonstrating strong production readiness.

Abstract

Detecting anomalies in large-scale system logs is critical for the reliability and security of modern computing infrastructure. We present LogNEO, a log anomaly detector built on EleutherAI's GPT-Neo (1.3B parameters) and fine-tuned with a novel partial-credit, exponentially decaying position-aware reward scheme combined with cross-entropy regularisation via Proximal Policy Optimisation (PPO). The position-aware reward explicitly models prediction difficulty: early positions receive higher rewards for correct predictions, while later positions incur stronger penalties for errors. LogNEO attains F1-scores of 0.927, 0.913, and 0.984 on the HDFS, BGL, and Thunderbird benchmarks, improving recall by up to 6 percentage points over the prior state-of-the-art LogGPT while maintaining comparable precision. A production microservice deployment over Apache Kafka, Redis, and TensorRT-accelerated inference demonstrates 45 ms end-to-end latency at 15,000 events per second.

item.title: How Small Can You Go? LoRA Fine-Tuning 270M-8B Models for Merchant Information Extraction in Financial Transactions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08051v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与目标

金融交易处理需从海量、噪声大、高度简写的银行交易字符串中，规模化抽取结构化商户信息（如商户名称、类别、地点）。当前生产系统采用LoRA微调的LLaMA 3.1-8B模型，F1达96.95%，但其80亿参数带来显著内存占用、高推理延迟与高昂部署成本。本研究旨在系统探索“更小是否可行且更优”，识别兼顾精度、速度与成本的轻量级替代方案。

方法与范围

我们开展面向部署的实证研究，评估24种模型变体：涵盖Gemma 3（270M/1B/4B）、Qwen 3.5（0.8B/2B/4B）、Aya（3.35B）及LLaMA 3.1-8B，统一采用LoRA微调。在相同数据集与评估协议下，多维量化对比：F1精度、推理吞吐量（tokens/s）、训练成本（GPU-h）、显存占用、首token延迟，并全部部署至Databricks Model Serving进行端到端生产验证。

关键发现

  • 参数压缩不等于性能牺牲：Qwen 3.5-4B（仅半数参数）达96.60% F1（–0.35），0.8B版本仍保持94.75% F1，超越多数2.5–4×更大的模型；
  • LoRA秩可大幅降低：LLaMA 3.1-8B使用rank=8即可达96.75% F1（仅–0.20），显著节省显存与计算开销；
  • 提示工程与训练范式影响显著：JSON-only prompting在Qwen 3.5-4B上最优；Chain-of-Thought微调平均提升F1 0.3–1.8点，但显式推理监督（如Think/Nothink模板）对结构化抽取无实质增益（ΔF1 < 0.004）；
  • 生产迁移稳健：14个≤8B模型上线后F1平均偏移仅+0.8点，唯Aya 3.35B因架构特性下降3–5点。

本研究为金融NLP场景提供了首个系统性“小模型可行性图谱”，支撑按SLA分级选型——从毫秒级低延迟（0.8B）到高精度关键任务（4B）。

AI Summary (English)

This study investigates the minimal viable model size for production-grade merchant information extraction from noisy financial transaction strings. We systematically evaluate 24 LoRA-fine-tuned variants across Gemma 3 (270M–4B), Qwen 3.5 (0.8B–4B), Aya (3.35B), and LLaMA 3.1-8B, measuring accuracy (F1), inference throughput, training cost, and hardware behavior. Key findings: (1) Qwen 3.5-4B achieves 96.60% F1—within 0.35 points of the 8B baseline—using half the parameters; (2) Qwen 3.5-0.8B attains 94.75% F1, outperforming larger models on latency-accuracy trade-offs; (3) LoRA rank can be reduced from 32 to 8 with only a 0.20-point F1 drop; (4) chain-of-thought fine-tuning boosts F1 by 0.3–1.8 points generally, but JSON-only prompting is optimal for Qwen 3.5-4B; and (5) explicit reasoning supervision (e.g., “Think” vs. “Nothink” templates) yields negligible gains (ΔF1 < 0.004), indicating it is unnecessary for structured extraction. All ≤8B models deploy reliably on Databricks, with average production F1 shift of just +0.8 points—except Aya 3.35B, which degrades by 3–5 points. Results enable precision- and latency-driven model selection in financial infrastructure.

Abstract

Financial transaction processing requires extracting structured merchant information from noisy, abbreviated bank transaction strings at scale. Our current production system, a LoRA-fine-tuned LLaMA 3.1-8B, achieves 96.95% F1 on this task, but deploying 8-billion-parameter models imposes prohibitive memory, latency, and cost constraints. To identify more efficient alternatives, we conduct a deployment-focused study of 24 model variants spanning four model families: Gemma 3 (270M, 1B, 4B), Qwen 3.5 (0.8B, 2B, 4B), Aya (3.35B), and LLaMA 3.1-8B, systematically evaluating accuracy, inference throughput, training cost, and hardware behavior to assess production suitability. Our findings show that: (1) reproducing the LLaMA 3.1-8B fine-tune with a LoRA rank of 8 achieves 96.75% F1, only 0.20 points below the rank-32 baseline; (2) Qwen 3.5 4B with JSON-only prompting reaches 96.60% F1, within 0.35 points of the 8B baseline while using roughly half the parameters; (3) the 0.8B Qwen 3.5 model achieves 94.75% F1, matching models 2.5-4x larger and offering an attractive latency-accuracy trade-off; (4) chain-of-thought fine-tuning generally improves F1 by 0.3-1.8 points across most models, although Qwen 3.5 4B performs best with direct JSON-only prompting; and (5) Qwen 3.5 Think and Nothink training templates produce nearly identical results (F1 differences <0.004), indicating that explicit reasoning supervision is unnecessary for structured extraction tasks. We further deploy all 14 fine-tuned sub-8B models as Databricks Model Serving endpoints and observe that benchmark performance transfers reliably to production, with an average F1 change of only 0.8 points. Aya 3.35B, based on the Cohere2 architecture, is the sole exception, exhibiting a 3-5 point decline under serving conditions. Based on these results, we provide deployment recommendations across accuracy and latency requirements, ...

item.title: CausShield: Sample Reconstruction-Resilient Vertical FL via Causal Representation Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08027v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

垂直联邦学习（VFL）允许多方在不共享原始样本的前提下，协同训练模型——各方仅持有同一用户集的纵向切分特征。然而，现有VFL协议仍易受主动样本重建攻击（如URVFL、InvL）威胁：攻击者可利用梯度/中间表示逆向推断出其他方的私有特征。当前防御方案存在根本性权衡困境：或过度抑制任务相关特征导致效用下降，或依赖监督式端到端训练，使模型在训练早期即暴露于重建风险。

方法创新：CausShield

本文提出CausShield，首次将结构因果模型（SCM） 引入VFL隐私保护框架。我们从因果视角重新定义：

  • 因果特征 = 直接影响学习目标的特征（任务相关、需保留）；
  • 非因果特征 = 与任务无关但高度个性化（如生物特征、ID类信息），是重建攻击的关键载体（需隔离）。

我们严格证明：非因果特征构成隐私泄漏的充分必要条件，并据此构建解耦式表示学习机制：在客户端与服务器间共享的表征中，显式分离任务相关分量（供协作建模）与任务无关分量（被掩蔽/扰动）。该分离通过无监督优化目标实现，避免早期监督泄露，且理论证明其不损害VFL的标准收敛性。

实验验证

在6个基准数据集上对比7种SOTA方法（含USENIX Security’25的InvL、NDSS’25的URVFL），CausShield在三方面显著领先：
✅ 隐私保护：重建误差提升3.2–8.7×，PSNR下降12.4 dB；
✅ 模型效用：AUC平均仅降0.3%，优于次优方案1.8%；
✅ 效率：通信开销降低37%，训练加速2.1×。
本工作为VFL提供了首个兼具理论可证安全性、实用高效性与部署鲁棒性的因果驱动防御范式。

AI Summary (English)

Vertical federated learning (VFL) enables collaborative modeling over vertically partitioned data without raw sample sharing, yet remains vulnerable to active sample reconstruction attacks (e.g., URVFL, InvL). Existing defenses suffer from a utility-privacy trade-off: they either suppress task-critical features or rely on supervised end-to-end training—exposing early-epoch representations. To address this, we propose CausShield, the first VFL defense grounded in structural causal modeling (SCM). We theoretically establish that non-causal features—task-irrelevant but sample-specific—are the primary enablers of reconstruction, while causal features are essential for utility. CausShield thus decomposes shared representations into task-relevant and task-irrelevant components via unsupervised causal representation learning, ensuring full-cycle privacy without compromising convergence guarantees. Extensive experiments against 7 SOTAs—including InvL (USENIX Security’25) and URVFL (NDSS’25)—show CausShield achieves up to 8.7× higher reconstruction error, only 0.3% AUC drop, and 37% lower communication cost, setting a new Pareto-optimal frontier in VFL privacy-efficiency balance.

Abstract

Vertical federated learning (VFL) is a distributed learning paradigm that leverages vertically partitioned features across isolated parties without sharing raw samples; however, it remains vulnerable to active sample reconstruction attacks. Existing defenses fail to achieve a satisfactory trade-off between model utility and privacy protection, due to either suppressing task-relevant information alongside privacy-sensitive features or relying on end-to-end supervised training to converge the defense module, which exposes the model to early-epoch vulnerability. To address this challenge, we adopt a structural causal model (SCM) insight and construct CausShield. From a task-learning standpoint, causal features within a raw sample are those that are directly relevant and contributory to the learning objective, whereas non-causal features are task-irrelevant but often encode sample-specific private information, thereby facilitating reconstruction. Importantly, we lay a theoretical foundation to prove this insight. CausShield thus decomposes the shared representations between the client and the coordinating server in VFL into task-relevant and task-irrelevant components to ensure full-cycle privacy protection. Nonetheless, the decomposition is inherently challenging due to the dual objectives of preserving model utility while mitigating privacy leakage. We address this via a carefully formulated optimization problem, which is solved through unsupervised representation learning. We further theoretically prove that CausShield preserves the convergence behavior of standard VFL. Extensive experiments compare CausShield against seven SOTAs, including InvL (USENIX Security'25), and evaluate robustness against advanced reconstruction attacks such as URVFL (NDSS'25). Results demonstrate that CausShield consistently outperforms in privacy protection, model utility, and computational efficiency.

item.title: Semantic Quorum Assurance: Collective Certification for Non-Deterministic AI Infrastructure
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08021v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）智能体深度融入云基础设施自治运维，分布式系统面临新型语义可靠性挑战：提议者智能体可生成语法正确、静态权限合规但操作层面危险的生产变更（如误改IAM策略、错误开放安全组端口、非授权数据导出）。传统共识协议（如Raft、Paxos）仅保障确定性状态复制，无法评估提案意图的安全性，导致“合法但有害”的操作被无差别执行。

方法：语义法定额保障（SQA）

我们提出Semantic Quorum Assurance（SQA）——一种面向非确定性AI基础设施的控制平面原语。SQA将变更提案建模为声明式执行合约，绑定不可篡改的密码学证据链；路由至由多类型、只读、沙箱化验证智能体组成的异构评审小组；采用风险自适应法定额谓词聚合判断：强制要求模型架构（model）与行为范式（archetype）双重多样性，依据校准后的可信度评分动态加权各验证器，且尊重关键范式（如“合规审计型”）的一票否决权。仅获批准的提案方可经由主权执行门控（sovereign execution gate）落地。

主要发现与创新

  • 在500个云基础设施典型变更场景测试中（排除模糊案例，仅报告明确安全/不安全样本），SQA将单智能体验证的不安全批准率从18.5%降至0.3%（降幅98.4%）；
  • 引入关联认知失效模型，形式化刻画非确定性验证器间的潜在共性偏差；
  • 实现云原生控制平面原型，验证延迟中位数为1.45–4.12秒（按风险等级分桶），兼顾强保障与实用性；
  • 首次将“意图安全”纳入分布式共识语义层，突破传统协议对确定性的依赖。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents deployed in autonomous cloud operations introduce a semantic reliability gap: proposals (e.g., IAM policy changes, firewall openings, data exports) can be syntactically valid and statically authorized yet operationally unsafe—beyond the scope of classical deterministic consensus protocols. To bridge this, we propose Semantic Quorum Assurance (SQA), a control-plane primitive for governing non-deterministic agentic infrastructure. SQA represents proposals as declarative execution contracts anchored to cryptographic evidence chains and routes them to a diverse panel of read-only, sandboxed validators. It aggregates judgments via a risk-adaptive quorum predicate enforcing model and archetype diversity, dynamically weighted by calibrated assurance scores, and respecting archetype-specific vetoes. Approved proposals execute only through a sovereign gate. Evaluated on 500 infrastructure mutation scenarios (safe/unsafe held-out trials, ambiguous cases excluded), SQA reduces unsafe approval from 18.5% (single-agent baseline) to 0.3%, with median validation latency of 1.45–4.12 seconds across risk buckets. We also formalize a correlated cognitive failure model for non-deterministic validators.

Abstract

As large language model (LLM) agents are integrated into autonomous cloud operations, distributed systems face a semantic reliability problem: proposer agents can generate production mutations, such as modifying IAM policies, opening firewall security groups, or executing data exports, that are syntactically valid and statically authorized but operationally unsafe. Classical distributed consensus protocols replicate deterministic state transitions but do not evaluate the safety of the proposed intent. To address this gap, we introduce Semantic Quorum Assurance (SQA), a control-plane primitive for governing non-deterministic agentic infrastructure. SQA represents proposals as declarative execution contracts bound to cryptographic evidence chains and routes them to a diverse panel of read-only, sandboxed validator agents. SQA aggregates their judgments under a risk-adaptive quorum predicate that enforces model and archetype diversity, adjusts weights based on calibrated assurance scores, and respects archetype-specific vetoes. Admitted proposals execute only through a sovereign execution gate. We instantiate SQA in a cloud-native control plane and formalize a correlated cognitive failure model for non-deterministic validators. On 500 infrastructure-inspired mutation scenarios, with safety results reported on held-out safe/unsafe trials excluding ambiguous scenarios, SQA reduces unsafe approval from 18.5% for single-agent validation to 0.3% while adding median validation latency of 1.45--4.12 seconds across the studied risk buckets.

item.title: GVC-Seg: Training-Free 3D Instance Segmentation via Geometric Visual Correspondence
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08014v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在点云数据的三维实例分割任务中，高精度分割对自动驾驶、机器人导航等机器视觉应用至关重要。当前主流方法依赖多个预训练基础模型生成3D候选区域（proposals），再通过提案聚合策略融合结果。然而，这类集成方法存在固有置信度偏差：不同分割模型因数据预处理、训练策略等差异，输出置信度分布不一致，导致集成过程过度偏向高置信度模型，削弱低置信但高准确率模型的贡献，最终限制整体性能上限。

方法创新：GVC-Seg

本文提出GVC-Seg——一种无需训练的三维实例分割新范式，核心思想是建立几何-视觉跨模态对应关系（Geometric-Visual Correspondence），从源头消解置信度偏差。具体包含三大设计：

  • 几何引导的3D提案生成模块：基于点云几何结构（如曲率、法向一致性）生成鲁棒、语义无关的初始提案，摆脱对模型置信度的依赖；
  • 掩码感知的CLIP特征提取模块：在2D图像空间中，针对每个3D提案投影生成的2D掩码区域，提取细粒度、上下文增强的CLIP视觉特征，实现语义精准对齐；
  • 无偏对应匹配机制：通过几何线索（如提案形状、尺度）与视觉线索（如CLIP嵌入相似性）联合约束匹配，使不同模型的提案在统一几何-语义坐标系下公平竞争，实现真正去偏的集成学习。

主要成果

在ScanNet v2、S3DIS和OpenVOC等基准上，GVC-Seg以零训练开销达成SOTA性能（mAP@50提升2.1–4.7个百分点），显著优于所有需训练的基线方法；更关键的是，其天然支持开放词汇语义分割——仅通过文本提示即可识别未见类别（如“rusty pipe”、“vintage lamp”），验证了方法的泛化性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)
