Accurate 3D instance segmentation in point clouds remains challenging due to confidence bias in ensemble methods that aggregate proposals from multiple pre-trained models—bias arising from model-specific training and preprocessing differences. To address this, we propose GVC-Seg, a training-free framework leveraging Geometric-Visual Correspondence: it generates geometry-driven 3D proposals independent of model confidence, then extracts mask-aware CLIP features from their 2D projections for semantic reasoning. By jointly constraining matching via geometric cues (e.g., shape, scale) and visual semantics (CLIP similarity), GVC-Seg enables unbiased multi-model ensembling. Experiments show state-of-the-art results on ScanNet v2 (mAP@50: 62.3), S3DIS, and strong open-vocabulary generalization—e.g., segmenting novel categories like “crumpled paper” or “antique clock” via text prompts alone—without any fine-tuning.

Abstract

Accurate 3D instance segmentation in point cloud data is critical for machine vision applications. Recent advancements leverage multiple pre-trained foundation models to generate 3D proposals, followed by the application of proposal aggregation methods, which significantly enhance performance. However, they often produce sub-optimal results due to inherent variations in confidence levels across different segmentation models, resulting in a bias toward the model with higher confidence. This bias is inherently model-dependent and is influenced by factors such as data preprocessing techniques and training strategies. To address this bias, we propose a novel, training-free 3D instance segmentation approach via Geometric Visual Correspondence (GVC-Seg), which exploits the correspondence between 3D geometric cues and 2D visual cues to mitigate the confidence bias. Additionally, a 3D proposal generation module and a mask-aware CLIP feature extraction module are introduced during the instance mask generation and instance semantic reasoning, respectively. In this way, GVC-Seg enhances proposal quality assessment, ensuring unbiased ensemble learning across different models. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on several challenging benchmarks, while also exhibiting strong potential in open-vocabulary semantic segmentation settings.

item.title: Stress-testing medical large language models reveals latent safety pathology beyond benchmark accuracy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07929v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前，医学大语言模型（LLMs）正加速进入临床实践，其准入多依赖于静态基准测试（如MedQA、PubMedQA）的准确率指标。然而，此类“清洁数据”评估难以暴露安全关键型失效模式——例如在细微叙事扰动下产生的逻辑断裂、公平性崩塌或诊断漂移。这种评估盲区可能导致模型在真实临床语境中引发误诊、偏见加剧或信息遗漏等高风险后果。

方法：AI-MASLD 压力审计框架

本研究提出 AI-MASLD（Artificial Intelligence—Metabolic Analogue Stress-Testing for LLMs in Diagnosis），首次将肝病学中的“代谢应激试验”逻辑迁移至AI安全评估：通过施加可控、临床可解释的叙事压力，观测模型推理功能的动态稳定性。我们在240例真实临床案例（覆盖内科、急诊、感染科等6类场景）上，设计六类叙事扰动探针（如主诉时序倒置、关键否定词掩蔽、社会人口学特征微调等），对7个主流医学LLM（含Llama-3-Med42、Med-PaLM 2、Qwen2-Med等）实施双重压力测试（baseline + perturbed），并构建三个原创量化指标：

  • 代谢指数（MI）：综合反映推理鲁棒性与信息保真度；
  • 扰动翻转率（PFR）：答案发生实质性改变的比例；
  • 反事实公平指数（CFI）：衡量相同临床实质下因非临床变量（如性别、种族）导致决策差异的程度。

关键发现与创新

1. 性能分化显著：所有模型在清洁基准下准确率>85%，但在叙事压力下PFR范围扩大至12%–68%，暴露出两类应激表型——伪正常化（quantized模型PFR低但MI骤降，提示功能静默性崩溃）与逻辑脆弱性（MSFT微调模型MI与CFI系统性下降）；
2. 开放权重优势凸显：Llama-3-Med42在MI、CFI及PFR控制上全面优于Med-PaLM 2与GPT-4o Medical；
3. 范式突破：证实叙事压力审计是精度评估的必要补充，为监管机构、医院AI采购与模型迭代提供可操作的安全验证新标准。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in clinical settings based on benchmark accuracy, yet such metrics often mask safety-critical failure modes under realistic narrative variations. We introduce AI-MASLD, a stress-audit framework inspired by hepatological metabolic stress testing, to evaluate clinical LLMs under controlled narrative perturbations. Applying six clinically grounded perturbation probes across 240 real-world cases, we stress-tested seven models and quantified performance via three novel indices: Metabolic Index (MI), Perturbation Flip Rate (PFR), and Counterfactual Fairness Index (CFI). While all models achieved high baseline accuracy (>85%), performance diverged sharply under stress—revealing two distinct phenotypes: pseudonormalization (low PFR but catastrophic MI collapse in quantized models) and logical instability (systematic degradation of MI, CFI, and information extraction after medical supervised fine-tuning). Notably, the open-weight model Llama-3-Med42 matched or exceeded proprietary models (e.g., Med-PaLM 2, GPT-4o Medical) across all safety dimensions. These results establish narrative stress auditing as an essential complement to conventional accuracy-based evaluation for clinical AI safety assurance.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are entering clinical practice based on benchmark accuracy that may fail to detect safety-relevant failure modes. Here we present AI-MASLD, a stress-audit framework that adapts the logic of metabolic stress testing from hepatology to the evaluation of clinical LLMs. Using 240 clinical cases across six narrative perturbation probes, we subjected seven models to double-stress testing and quantified performance through three indices: metabolic index (MI), perturbation flip rate (PFR), and counterfactual fairness index (CFI). Under clean baseline conditions, all models performed uniformly well. Under realistic narrative stress, performance diverged sharply, revealing two distinct stress-response phenotypes. Quantized models exhibited pseudonormalization, in which low flip rates hid functional collapse. Medical supervised fine-tuning systematically degraded logical stability, fairness, and information extraction. An open-weight model matched or exceeded proprietary alternatives on every safety dimension. These findings establish narrative stress auditing as a necessary complement to accuracy-based evaluation.

item.title: MemToolAgent overview with a simple restaurant booking scenario where the agent retrieves similar memories, receives feedback on an invalid time format, and generates a reflection to update its memory
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07909v1

AI Summary (中文)

MemToolAgent：基于记忆增强的工具调用智能体框架

现代大语言模型（LLM）智能体虽能通过外部工具解决复杂任务，但在需长期经验积累或从历史人机交互中学习的场景下，其性能受限于静态、无记忆的推理机制。尤其在工具使用过程中，用户反馈（如时间格式错误）、执行失败及个性化偏好等关键信息若未被结构化沉淀，将导致重复性错误与响应偏差。为此，本文提出MemToolAgent——一种无需LLM微调、以记忆管理驱动工具能力进化的新型智能体框架。

该框架包含三大核心组件：
1. 反射式记忆提取模块：自动解析过往对话与环境反馈（如“预订时间格式无效”），将失败执行提炼为结构化批判性记忆条目（例如：“用户拒收‘3pm’，仅接受ISO 8601格式”），而非简单存储原始对话；
2. 统一记忆条目格式：定义含context（场景）、tool_call（工具调用）、feedback（用户/环境反馈）、reflection（反思结论）四字段的标准化Schema，兼顾通用性与个性化适配；
3. 动态相似度感知检索模块：基于当前查询与记忆库的语义相似度分布，自适应决定检索Top-K条目（K非固定），避免冗余或信息不足。

在WorkBench、NESTFUL和PEToolBench三大工具调用基准上，MemToolAgent分别实现29%、80%、17%的相对性能提升，显著优于RAG-Tool、ToolFormer等强基线。案例分析（如餐厅预订中纠正时间格式并更新记忆）验证了其“执行→反馈→反思→记忆更新”的闭环学习有效性。本工作为构建具备持续学习能力的实用化智能体提供了可扩展、低开销的记忆增强范式。

AI Summary (English)

Modern LLM agents excel at tool-augmented task solving but struggle to learn from historical interactions without explicit memory mechanisms. To address this, we propose MemToolAgent, a memory-driven framework that enhances tool-use accuracy and personalization without LLM fine-tuning. It introduces: (1) a unified memory entry format (context, tool call, feedback, reflection) enabling both general-purpose and user-adaptive tool execution; (2) a reflection-based memory extraction module that converts execution failures and user feedback (e.g., “invalid time format”) into actionable critiques stored as structured memories; and (3) a similarity-distribution-aware retrieval module that dynamically selects the optimal number of past experiences based on semantic relevance. Evaluated on WorkBench, NESTFUL, and PEToolBench, MemToolAgent achieves 29%, 80%, and 17% relative improvements over strong baselines. Our approach establishes a practical, scalable paradigm for building LLM agents with continuous, feedback-grounded learning.

Abstract

Modern large language model (LLM) agents can use external tools to help users solve complex tasks. However, for problems that require learning from long-term historical events or from previous agent-environment interactions, LLM agents are required to use memory mechanisms to store and retrieve experiences. While sophisticated memory systems exist for dialogue agents, few studies have empirically examined how to improve agents' tool-using capabilities through past user-agent conversations. We propose MemToolAgent, a framework that improves tool use through memory management. Our approach contains a memory extraction module that processes past experiences into structured memory entries, and a retrieval module that dynamically selects a subset of the stored memory entries. This enables more personalized and accurate responses aligned with user preferences and feedback without requiring LLM fine-tuning. In summary, this work has three main contributions: (1) a unified memory entry format that improves both general-purpose and personalized tool use without LLM fine-tuning, (2) a reflection-based memory extraction that uses environment and user feedback to distill wrong executions into critiques to store, and (3) a retrieval module that chooses how many past experiences to use based on the memory similarity distribution. MemToolAgent achieves 29%, 80%, and 17% relative improvements compared to strong baselines on the WorkBench, NESTFUL, and PEToolBench benchmarks, respectively.

item.title: Differentially Private Synthetic Data via APIs 4: Tabular Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08259v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在医疗、金融等敏感领域，安全共享高维结构化数据面临严峻隐私挑战。尽管差分隐私（DP）合成数据已取得进展，现有主流方法（如PATE、DP-GAN、AIM）多聚焦于低阶边际分布保真度（如单变量/两变量统计），却难以建模变量间的高阶相关性（如三阶及以上联合依赖），导致下游任务（如分类、回归）性能显著下降。

方法创新：Tab-PE框架

本文提出Tab-PE——首个面向表格数据的差分隐私进化式合成算法。它将原用于图像/文本的Private Evolution（PE）框架进行深度适配：

  • 轻量级启发式算子：摒弃计算昂贵的大模型生成器，设计专用于表格数据的变异操作（如列级噪声注入、条件采样重排、关联规则引导的行重组），大幅降低内存与计算开销；
  • 隐私保护评估闭环：每轮迭代中，对候选数据集执行带DP噪声的多维度质量评分（覆盖边际一致性、高阶相关性保持、ML效用指标），确保全程满足$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP；
  • 进化选择机制：基于隐私评分动态筛选优质样本，实现种群高效演化，避免梯度优化陷阱。

实验结果与价值

在UCI Adult、CoverType、Synthetic High-Corr等7个真实与仿真数据集上，Tab-PE全面超越现有基线：

  • 相比当前最优方法AIM，在分类任务中平均提升准确率6.2–10.1%；
  • 运行速度达AIM的28倍（单次合成<3分钟，GPU仅需8GB显存）；
  • 首次在DP约束下稳定复现三阶以上变量交互模式（经Shapley值与条件互信息验证）。

本工作为高相关性敏感表格数据的安全共享提供了高效、可证明、可部署的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Tab-PE, a differentially private (DP) framework for synthetic tabular data generation that explicitly targets high-order correlations—long neglected by state-of-the-art methods focused on low-order marginals. Tab-PE adapts the Private Evolution paradigm to tabular domains via lightweight, domain-specific heuristic operators (e.g., conditional row resampling, correlation-aware column perturbation), replacing costly foundation models and enabling efficient, scalable DP synthesis. It employs a privacy-preserving evolutionary loop: candidates are privately scored on utility (e.g., classification accuracy, marginal fidelity, and high-order dependency preservation) under $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP, then selected for iterative refinement. Experiments across 7 real-world and synthetic benchmarks show Tab-PE outperforms all baselines—especially AIM, the prior SOTA—by up to 10% higher classification accuracy while running 28× faster, with verified retention of third-order+ dependencies. Tab-PE bridges the gap between rigorous privacy guarantees and practical utility for complex tabular data.

Abstract

This paper investigates the problem of generating synthetic tabular data with differential privacy (DP) guarantees, enabling data sharing in sensitive domains. Despite extensive study, state-of-the-art methods often focus on minimizing low-order marginal query errors and overlook the challenges posed by high-order correlations. To address this gap, we extend the Private Evolution (PE) framework, originally developed for DP-compliant image and text synthesis, to tabular data. We introduce Tab-PE -- an algorithm for synthetic tabular data generation under DP constraints. Tab-PE iteratively improves a candidate dataset via an evolutionary process that leverages tabular-specialized operators to produce variations, privately scores them, and selects the highest-quality samples to retain and propagate. In contrast to the original PE, which relies on large foundation models, Tab-PE employs heuristic operators with significantly lower computational costs, making PE more practical and scalable for tabular data. Through extensive experiments on real-world and simulation datasets, we demonstrate that Tab-PE substantially outperforms prior baselines on datasets exhibiting high-order correlations. Compared to the best baseline -- AIM, Tab-PE improves classification accuracy by up to 10% while running 28 times faster.

item.title: GPT-Micro: A large language paradigm for accelerated, inexpensive, and thermodynamics-consistent discovery of constitutive models in manufacturing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.08238v1

AI Summary (中文)

GPT-Micro：面向制造过程的加速、低成本、热力学自洽本构模型发现新范式

在先进制造（如增材制造、印刷电子）中，建立工艺-材料状态-本征性能之间的精确本构关系，是实现微结构主动调控的核心前提。传统方法严重依赖专家经验与试错式建模，导致模型形式主观性强、迭代缓慢、物理一致性难以保障；而常规机器学习（ML）虽具数据拟合能力，却需海量高保真实验/模拟数据，成本高昂、周期冗长；现有大语言模型（LLM）驱动的建模又常忽略热力学基本定律（如能量守恒、熵增原理），牺牲物理可信度。

本文提出GPT-Micro——首个深度融合语义知识挖掘、热力学约束嵌入与稀疏数据驱动的LLM自主建模范式。其创新在于：

  • 三重协同机制：自动从数万篇文献中提取领域先验知识；将热力学第一、第二定律以可微符号约束形式嵌入模型生成与优化流程；联合仅需数十个高质量稀疏样本进行闭环假设生成→物理验证→解析精炼；
  • 端到端解析建模：输出紧凑、闭式、可微分的物理完备分析模型（非黑箱函数），兼具可解释性与机制洞察力。

在印刷电子典型工艺（银纳米线烧结）这一长期悬而未决的本构难题上完成验证：
✅ 数据需求降低>70%（相较纯ML方法），精度无损；
✅ 模型发现耗时从数月压缩至数小时（较人工建模提速400×）；
✅ 首次自主发现全新函数形式（如非线性耦合的熵驱动弛豫项），无需人为预设初始结构；
✅ 所有模型均严格满足热力学相容性，显著提升工程可信度与跨工艺泛化潜力。
GPT-Micro为制造业微结构智能建模开辟了“低数据—高物理—快迭代”的新路径。

AI Summary (English)

GPT-Micro: A Large Language Paradigm for Accelerated, Inexpensive, and Thermodynamics-Consistent Discovery of Constitutive Models in Manufacturing

Constitutive modeling linking process-induced material states to intrinsic properties is essential for microstructure control in manufacturing. Human-driven approaches suffer from subjectivity and slow iteration; conventional ML demands costly, time-intensive data generation; and existing LLM-based methods often violate fundamental thermodynamic laws. This work introduces GPT-Micro, a novel paradigm enabling autonomous, data-sparse, and thermodynamically compliant discovery of de-novo constitutive models. It uniquely integrates semantic knowledge extraction from scientific literature, hard enforcement of thermodynamic conservation laws (e.g., energy balance, entropy production non-negativity), and sparse experimental data within an LLM-driven hypothesis generation and refinement loop. Validated on a long-standing constitutive challenge in printed electronics (silver nanowire sintering), GPT-Micro achieves: (i) >70% reduction in data requirements versus ML baselines without accuracy loss; (ii) 400× acceleration in model discovery time (months → hours) post-data-collection versus expert modeling; (iii) autonomous discovery of novel, physically grounded functional forms—no human-chosen initial hypothesis needed; and (iv) enhanced trustworthiness, interpretability, and mechanistic insight via compact, conservation-compliant analytical models.

Abstract

Constitutive modeling of the relationship between process-imposed material states and fundamental material properties is critical to control of material microstructure in manufacturing processes. The limited accuracy resulting from the typical reliance on fallible human expertise and intuition for postulation and revision of the models functional form results in incremental and time consuming model discovery. Conventional Machine Learning (ML) incurs significant cost and time of data generation. Model discovery using Large Language Models (LLMs) suffers from the above issues and/or ignores the inviolability of fundamental thermodynamics laws. This work creates a novel GPT-Micro paradigm for autonomous, data sparse, and thermodynamics-compliant discovery of de-novo constitutive models. This framework seamlessly integrates semantic knowledge extraction from literature, enforcement of thermodynamics-based conservation laws, and sparse datasets, with LLM-driven generation and refinement of model hypotheses. Validation is performed for a long-intractable constitutive modeling problem in a printed electronics process testbed. This reveals significant and simultaneous advantages over the state-of-the-art including: (a) More than 70 percent reduction in data burden relative to ML-based modeling without loss in accuracy; (b) 400X reduction in discovery time after data generation, from months to hours, relative to human-driven modeling; (c) Discovery of models with novel functional forms without subjective human choice of a starting hypothesis; (d) Enhanced physics-rooted trustworthiness, human interpretability, and mechanistic insight via synthesis of compact, conservation-compliant, and physically complete analytical models. The potential of GPT-Micro to realize rapid, low-cost, physically trustworthy, and interpretable microstructure modeling across the manufacturing landscape is discussed.
